module.label: Pediatric limp, hot joint, injury, or non-weight bearing

use_when: Child or adolescent with limp, refusal to walk, non-weight-bearing, hot or swollen joint, suspected septic arthritis, limb pain, musculoskeletal injury, localized bone tenderness, persistent limp after trauma, or age-specific hip/knee pain concern.

Pediatric musculoskeletal workup needs age-band routing, current weight-bearing status, fever and infection features, joint range-of-motion localization, injury and safeguarding review, imaging stewardship, and urgent orthopaedic or pediatric escalation when septic arthritis or bone infection is possible.
do_not_use_when: Adult patient; use the adult musculoskeletal, back pain, trauma, or rheumatology pathway as appropriate.
Unstable child with shock, sepsis, altered mental status, major trauma, neurovascular compromise, or limb-threatening injury; escalate immediately while documenting focused findings.

Primary abdominal, genitourinary, neurologic, or hematologic presentation without limp, limb pain, hot joint, injury, or weight-bearing change; use the source-specific pediatric pathway and add this module only if musculoskeletal findings change management.
Known oncology, hemophilia, sickle-cell crisis, postoperative orthopedics, or complex rheumatology protocol where a specialty pathway overrides this general pediatric limp and hot-joint module.

modifier_add_on_policy.rationale: This validated pediatric musculoskeletal pack already includes fever, septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, sepsis, wound-source, and urgent orthopaedic routing; generic infection modifier prompts should not be activated by its own hot-joint aliases.

triggers: child hot swollen knee cannot bear weight
child hot swollen joint cannot bear weight
child hot swollen knee can't bear weight
child cannot bear weight after injury

differentialBuckets.label: Septic arthritis or osteomyelitis until excluded
differentialBuckets.action: Treat fever, limp, swelling, reduced range of motion, or limited limb use as possible septic arthritis or osteomyelitis until senior review and objective infection assessment make serious infection unlikely.
differentialBuckets.rationale: Septic arthritis is an orthopaedic emergency, and bone or joint infection can cause life-threatening illness and long-term disability.

source.source_section: key points, diagnosis, investigations, treatment, and consultation
source.implementation_notes: Use local pediatric sepsis and orthopaedic pathways immediately when the child is unwell.

differentialBuckets.diagnostic_target: Septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, myositis, discitis, epidural collection, or soft-tissue source with bacteremia.
differentialBuckets.management_change: Changes urgency of orthopaedic and pediatric consultation, blood culture and inflammatory-marker testing, joint aspiration, imaging, antibiotics, admission, and transfer.
differentialBuckets.likelihood_ratio_note: Guidelines do not assign one universal LR to the broad septic-joint or bone-infection frame; weight-bearing ability, fever, range of motion, labs, imaging, and aspiration findings are interpreted together.
differentialBuckets.limitations: Normal inflammatory markers or early X-ray findings do not reliably exclude early or small-bone infection.

differentialBuckets.label: Fracture, occult injury, or safeguarding concern
differentialBuckets.action: Clarify mechanism, timing, witnessed injury, pain location, bruising pattern, and whether persistent non-weight-bearing continues despite analgesia or a negative initial X-ray.
differentialBuckets.rationale: Trauma and toddler fractures are common, while inflicted injury and occult fracture must be considered when history, developmental ability, or examination findings do not fit.

source.source_section: differential diagnosis by age, assessment, investigations, and consultation
source.implementation_notes: Pair injury localization with local child-safety policy when non-accidental injury is plausible.

differentialBuckets.diagnostic_target: Toddler fracture, growth-plate injury, sprain, ligament injury, occult fracture, dislocation, or non-accidental injury.
differentialBuckets.management_change: Changes imaging site, immobilization, analgesia, orthopaedic review, safety-team involvement, discharge instructions, and follow-up interval.
differentialBuckets.likelihood_ratio_note: LR data are not encoded for the broad injury frame because the needed test depends on localized site, mechanism, age, and exam findings.
differentialBuckets.limitations: A single normal radiograph may not end the workup when the child remains unable to bear weight or the story is inconsistent.

differentialBuckets.label: Lower-risk transient synovitis or self-limited limp after danger features are absent
differentialBuckets.action: Classify as lower risk only when the child is systemically well, afebrile, ambulating comfortably or improving with analgesia, and high-risk diagnoses have been considered unlikely.
differentialBuckets.rationale: Most children with limp do not require investigation, but discharge requires comfortable ambulation, a clear working diagnosis, and follow-up.

source.source_section: key points, investigations, consultation, and discharge criteria
source.implementation_notes: Do not use this bucket to reassure a child with acute inability to walk or fever with joint limitation.

differentialBuckets.diagnostic_target: Transient synovitis, acute myositis, mild sprain, minor trauma, or other self-limited limp.
differentialBuckets.management_change: Changes need for observation, analgesia response, investigations, discharge readiness, and follow-up plan.
differentialBuckets.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not assigned to a low-risk discharge frame; it depends on absence of source-defined red flags and a reliable follow-up plan.
differentialBuckets.limitations: Low-risk classification is unsafe if the cause is unclear and the child is not walking comfortably or follow-up is not secure.

differentialBuckets.label: Age-specific hip or referred knee pathology
differentialBuckets.action: Consider age-specific diagnoses such as developmental dysplasia, Perthes disease, slipped upper femoral epiphysis, traction apophysitis, and stress injury when hip, groin, thigh, or knee pain localizes the limp.
differentialBuckets.rationale: The differential diagnosis of a limp changes by age, and hip disease can present as knee or thigh pain.

source.source_section: common differential diagnoses by age and pain referred to joints above and below
source.implementation_notes: Adolescent hip, groin, thigh, or referred knee pain should not be treated as a generic sprain.

differentialBuckets.diagnostic_target: DDH, Perthes disease, slipped upper femoral epiphysis, apophysitis, stress fracture, or referred hip pathology.
differentialBuckets.management_change: Changes need for pelvic or site-specific imaging, weight-bearing restriction, orthopaedic referral, and activity guidance.
differentialBuckets.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not encoded because source guidance routes by age, site, and examination localization rather than a single bedside test.
differentialBuckets.limitations: Knee pain can be referred from hip pathology, especially in adolescents; exam the joint above and below the pain site.

differentialBuckets.label: Systemic, hematologic, rheumatologic, neurologic, or referred cause
differentialBuckets.action: Ask for night pain, weight loss, pallor, bruising, morning stiffness, neurologic symptoms, abdominal or genitourinary symptoms, and recurrent or persistent limp.
differentialBuckets.rationale: Limp can reflect malignancy, inflammatory arthritis, hematologic disease, neurologic disease, intra-abdominal pathology, genitourinary pathology, or vitamin deficiency rather than isolated musculoskeletal injury.

source.source_section: red flags, differential diagnosis, and referred causes
source.implementation_notes: Route to pediatric abdominal, GU, neuro, hematology, or rheumatology workup when non-MSK symptoms dominate.

differentialBuckets.diagnostic_target: Leukemia or bone tumor, inflammatory arthritis, vasculitis, hemophilia, sickle-cell vaso-occlusive crisis, Guillain-Barre syndrome, abdominal pathology, GU pathology, or nutritional deficiency.
differentialBuckets.management_change: Changes CBC and film, inflammatory markers, specialty referral, abdominal/GU/neuro add-on, admission, and follow-up urgency.
differentialBuckets.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not encoded for this broad systemic frame; it is a routing guard to prevent anchoring on benign injury when systemic features are present.
differentialBuckets.limitations: This module does not replace specialty hematology, oncology, rheumatology, neurologic, abdominal, or GU pathways once those features dominate.

safetyChecks.label: Measure temperature
safetyChecks.action: Measure and document current temperature with age-appropriate method.
safetyChecks.rationale: Fever changes a limp or hot joint into a possible septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, or sepsis pathway.
safetyChecks.management_change: Fever increases urgency for senior review, infection labs, cultures, imaging, orthopaedic consultation, antibiotics, and admission decisions.
safetyChecks.options: ___ C/F / Febrile / Afebrile / Hypothermic / Not available
safetyChecks.limitations: Antipyretics and recent measurement timing can mask fever trajectory.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to temperature as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: key points and septic arthritis red flag
source.implementation_notes: Keep temperature in baseline data, not physical exam maneuvers.

safetyChecks.label: Measure heart rate
safetyChecks.action: Measure heart rate and interpret with age, fever, pain, hydration, perfusion, and sepsis context.
safetyChecks.rationale: Tachycardia can reflect pain, fever, dehydration, sepsis, or shock in a child with limp or bone/joint infection.
safetyChecks.management_change: Abnormal heart rate changes urgency, observation, fluid/sepsis pathway, analgesia reassessment, and disposition.
safetyChecks.options: ___ bpm / Normal for age / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available
safetyChecks.limitations: Pain, anxiety, fever, crying, and medications can alter pediatric heart rate.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to heart rate as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: assessment and escalation for serious pathology
source.implementation_notes: Use age-adjusted vital-sign interpretation.

safetyChecks.label: Measure respiratory rate
safetyChecks.action: Count respiratory rate and interpret with age, fever, pain, sepsis, and respiratory source context.
safetyChecks.rationale: Tachypnea can be an early marker of systemic illness or sepsis in a child with suspected bone or joint infection.
safetyChecks.management_change: Abnormal respiratory rate changes sepsis escalation, oxygen support, transfer threshold, and whether respiratory-source evaluation is added.
safetyChecks.options: ___/min / Normal for age / Tachypneic / Bradypneic / Labored / Not available
safetyChecks.limitations: Crying or distress can distort the count; repeat when calm if safe.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to respiratory rate as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: assessment and escalation for serious pathology
source.implementation_notes: Keep respiratory rate in baseline safety data.

safetyChecks.label: Measure blood pressure
safetyChecks.action: Measure blood pressure with appropriate cuff size when systemic illness, sepsis, major pain, trauma, or disposition concern is present.
safetyChecks.rationale: Hypotension is a late and dangerous sign in pediatric sepsis or shock and changes transfer and resuscitation decisions.
safetyChecks.management_change: Abnormal blood pressure changes immediate escalation, fluids, senior review, and transfer threshold.
safetyChecks.options: ___/___ mmHg / Normal for age / Low / High / Not available
safetyChecks.limitations: Wrong cuff size, movement, anxiety, and pain can distort pediatric blood pressure.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to blood pressure as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: management and seriously unwell or septic child consideration
source.implementation_notes: Escalate per local pediatric sepsis policy when abnormal.

safetyChecks.label: Measure oxygen saturation
safetyChecks.action: Measure pulse oximetry when the child is unwell, tachypneic, febrile, sedated after analgesia, or needs escalation.
safetyChecks.rationale: Hypoxemia suggests systemic illness or an alternate respiratory/sepsis source and changes transfer readiness.
safetyChecks.management_change: Low oxygen saturation changes oxygen support, senior review, respiratory-source add-on, and transfer threshold.
safetyChecks.options: ___% room air / Normal / Low / On oxygen / Not available
safetyChecks.limitations: Poor perfusion, motion, nail products, and probe fit can reduce accuracy.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to oxygen saturation as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: escalation and transfer considerations
source.implementation_notes: Use as baseline safety data for unwell children.

safetyChecks.label: Document mental status
safetyChecks.action: Document alertness, interaction, consolability, confusion, lethargy, or toxicity before focusing on limb findings.
safetyChecks.rationale: Altered mental status or toxic appearance moves a limp/hot-joint presentation toward emergency sepsis or systemic illness escalation.
safetyChecks.management_change: Abnormal mental status changes resuscitation priority, antibiotics, transfer, and whether bedside documentation can wait.
safetyChecks.options: Alert / Irritable / Lethargic / Confused / Toxic appearing / Not assessed
safetyChecks.findings_options: Alert and interactive at baseline / Irritable but consolable / Lethargic or unusually sleepy / Confused or not acting normally / Toxic appearing / Unable to assess baseline
safetyChecks.limitations: Developmental stage, pain, sleep, sedating medications, and neurodevelopmental baseline can affect interpretation.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to mental status as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: management and seriously unwell or septic child consideration
source.implementation_notes: Keep mental status in baseline safety data, not physical exam maneuvers.

safetyChecks.label: Measure current weight
safetyChecks.action: Measure or verify current weight before weight-based analgesia, antibiotics, fluids, sedation, transfer, or imaging planning.
safetyChecks.rationale: Pediatric analgesia, antimicrobial dosing, fluids, procedural planning, and transfer communication depend on current weight.
safetyChecks.management_change: Current weight changes analgesia dosing, IV antibiotic dosing, fluid bolus calculations, sedation/imaging planning, and safe handoff.
safetyChecks.options: ___ kg / Parent-reported / Measured today / Estimated only / Not available
safetyChecks.limitations: Estimated weight can misdose antibiotics or analgesia; use measured weight when feasible.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to weight as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: antibiotic treatment and pediatric dosing context
source.implementation_notes: Keep current weight in baseline data, not physical exam maneuvers.

safetyChecks.label: Measure pain score
safetyChecks.action: Measure pain using an age-appropriate pain scale and document response to analgesia when reassessing gait or range of motion.
safetyChecks.rationale: Pain severity and analgesia response help separate safe reassessment from persistent severe pain requiring imaging, consultation, or admission.
safetyChecks.management_change: Severe or persistent pain changes analgesia, imaging, observation, admission, and whether discharge is safe.
safetyChecks.options: 0 to 10 / Faces scale / FLACC / Severe / Improved after analgesia / Not available
safetyChecks.findings_options: Age-appropriate score documented / Mild pain / Moderate pain / Severe pain / Improved after analgesia / Unable to score
safetyChecks.limitations: Young age, neurodevelopmental differences, fear, and language can make pain scoring imprecise.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to pain score as baseline clinical data.

source.source_section: investigations and discharge after analgesia and comfortable ambulation
source.implementation_notes: Pain score is baseline clinical data and should not be listed as a physical exam maneuver.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask pediatric MSK age and walking baseline
requiredQuestions.text: What is the child's exact age and usual walking or mobility baseline?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask first for every pediatric limp, hot joint, injury, or non-weight-bearing concern.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Age and baseline mobility change the differential diagnosis, expected gait, imaging choice, and discharge threshold.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Age-band and baseline changes whether the workup prioritizes toddler fracture, transient synovitis, Perthes disease, slipped upper femoral epiphysis, infection, malignancy, or neurodevelopmental baseline comparison.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: under 3 months / 3 to 35 months / 3 to 10 years / over 10 years / non-ambulatory baseline / ambulatory baseline / mobility aid / developmental baseline differs
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; age band is used for source-defined routing and differential diagnosis.

source.source_section: common differential diagnoses by age
source.implementation_notes: Make age band visible rather than applying adult MSK logic to children.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask current weight-bearing ability
requiredQuestions.text: Can the child walk or bear weight now after appropriate analgesia?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask before reassurance, discharge, or low-risk classification.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Acute inability to walk or weight bear is a red flag and can indicate infection, fracture, or other serious pathology.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Persistent inability to bear weight changes urgency of imaging, senior review, observation, orthopaedic advice, and discharge safety.
requiredQuestions.options: Endorsed able to walk comfortably / Denied unable to bear weight / Limping but weight-bearing / Improved after analgesia / Worse / Unsure
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; guidelines treat acute inability to walk or weight bear as a red flag across pediatric causes.

source.source_section: key points and discharge criteria
source.implementation_notes: Document the answer as an endorsed or denied functional status.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask onset and injury mechanism
requiredQuestions.text: When did symptoms start and was there a witnessed fall, twist, direct blow, puncture, or other injury?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask for every child with limp, limb pain, or inability to bear weight.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Onset and mechanism localize traumatic injury, occult fracture, penetrating source, and non-accidental injury risk.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Mechanism changes imaging site, immobilization, wound-source assessment, safeguarding pathway, and follow-up urgency.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: no injury / fall / twist / direct blow / puncture wound / sports injury / unwitnessed / inconsistent history / delayed presentation
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; mechanism directs site-specific imaging and safeguarding review.

source.source_section: assessment and differential including trauma and child abuse
source.implementation_notes: Avoid generic adult injury labels; make mechanism and timing explicit.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask pain location and referred pain
requiredQuestions.text: Where is the pain or limp best localized, including hip, groin, thigh, knee, ankle, foot, back, abdomen, or scrotum?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask to localize the joint or non-MSK source before choosing exam maneuvers or imaging.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Pain can be referred from joints above or below, spine, abdomen, GU tract, or hip pathology presenting as knee pain.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Location changes site-specific exam, X-ray or ultrasound selection, adolescent hip pathway, abdominal/GU add-on, and urgent surgical routing.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: hip / groin / thigh / knee / ankle / foot / back / abdomen / scrotum / diffuse / child cannot localize
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; source guidance uses localization to determine imaging and add-on pathways.

source.source_section: background and assessment of joints above and below and abdomen
source.implementation_notes: Location options are explicitly multi-select because children may have referred or poorly localized pain.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask fever and infection symptoms
requiredQuestions.text: Has the child had fever, rigors, recent bacterial infection, skin wound, puncture injury, or reduced limb use without clear trauma?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when a hot joint, limp, swelling, reduced range of motion, or non-weight-bearing concern is present.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Fever and infection-source history raise concern for septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, myositis, or sepsis.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Positive findings change need for blood culture, FBC, ESR, CRP, imaging, urgent orthopaedic/pediatric review, and antibiotics.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: fever endorsed / fever denied / rigors / recent infection / skin wound / puncture injury / reduced limb use / no infection symptoms / unsure
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; fever plus joint swelling and reduced range of motion is treated as source-defined escalation.

source.source_section: key points, diagnosis, and potential sources
source.implementation_notes: Keep source-localizing infection questions in history, not in a generic fever bundle.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask swelling and motion limitation
requiredQuestions.text: Is there visible swelling, redness, warmth, severe pain with movement, or reduced active use of the limb or joint?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, fracture, or inflammatory arthritis is possible.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Swelling, warmth, and reduced range of motion help identify joint infection, fracture, inflammatory arthritis, or severe soft-tissue disease.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Positive findings change urgency of examination, imaging, inflammatory markers, aspiration, and orthopaedic discussion.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: swelling endorsed / swelling denied / redness / warmth / severe movement pain / reduced use / normal use / unsure
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; reduced range of motion is interpreted with fever, weight-bearing ability, labs, and imaging.

source.source_section: key points and septic arthritis features
source.implementation_notes: Use positive and negative controls for each symptom cluster.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask systemic and malignancy clues
requiredQuestions.text: Are night pain, persistent symptoms, weight loss, pallor, bruising, bleeding, morning stiffness, or recurrent fevers present?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when limp is persistent, unexplained, atraumatic, recurrent, or not improving.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Systemic symptoms can indicate malignancy, hematologic disease, inflammatory arthritis, vasculitis, or chronic infection.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Positive findings change CBC/film, inflammatory markers, admission, specialty referral, and follow-up urgency.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: night pain / persistent symptoms / weight loss / pallor / bruising / bleeding / morning stiffness / recurrent fever / all denied / unsure
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; systemic features require broader guideline-cited evaluation and avoid false reassurance.

source.source_section: differential diagnosis and red flags
source.implementation_notes: Escalate rather than documenting generic MSK reassurance when systemic features are present.

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask septic hip predictor context
conditionalQuestions.text: For hip pain or suspected septic arthritis, are fever, non-weight-bearing, elevated inflammatory markers, or severe passive-motion pain present?
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when hip pain, fever, effusion, septic arthritis, or transient synovitis is being considered.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Septic hip predictors and passive-motion pain help determine whether transient synovitis is unsafe as a working diagnosis.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Positive context changes urgency of orthopaedic consultation, ultrasound, MRI, aspiration, antibiotics, and admission.
conditionalQuestions.options: Multi-select: fever / non-weight-bearing / elevated CRP or ESR / elevated WBC / severe passive-motion pain / none / pending labs / unsure
conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available as a single app value; prediction-rule and test-characteristic data exist for some septic hip contexts, but age, lab availability, and joint site require cited guideline and local orthopaedic interpretation.

source.source_section: septic arthritis red flags and reference list for septic arthritis predictors
source.implementation_notes: Do not present prediction rules as definitive without local lab and orthopaedic interpretation.

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask adolescent hip slip symptoms
conditionalQuestions.text: For an adolescent, is there hip, groin, thigh, or referred knee pain with reduced activity or altered gait?
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when adolescent, teen, groin, thigh, knee, hip, or slipped upper femoral epiphysis terms are present.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Adolescent hip disease can present as knee or thigh pain and should not be treated as a generic sprain.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Positive findings change need for pelvis/hip imaging, weight-bearing restriction, orthopaedic advice, and activity restriction.
conditionalQuestions.options: Multi-select: hip pain / groin pain / thigh pain / knee pain / reduced activity / altered gait / no hip-type symptoms / unsure
conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; source guidance routes by age and localization.

source.source_section: differential diagnoses by age and referred pain
source.implementation_notes: Route adolescent hip and referred knee pain to child-specific imaging and orthopaedic review.

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask safeguarding and injury consistency
conditionalQuestions.text: Does the history fit the child's developmental ability, observed timing, injury pattern, and caregiver explanations?
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when fall, unwitnessed event, delayed presentation, bruising, recurrent injury, or inconsistent explanation is present.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Safeguarding review prevents occult or inflicted injury from being mislabeled as benign limp.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Concern changes need for senior review, child-safety policy, skeletal imaging strategy, documentation, and disposition.
conditionalQuestions.options: Fits development / Does not fit development / Unwitnessed / Delayed presentation / Recurrent injury / Concerning bruising / Explanation changed / Unsure
conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for safeguarding context; use local child protection policy and source-defined concern features.

source.source_section: differential including trauma and child abuse
source.implementation_notes: Keep safeguarding concern visible and avoid PHI-rich unsupported free-text exports.

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask back neurologic and bladder symptoms
conditionalQuestions.text: With back or neck pain, are weakness, numbness, gait change, urinary retention, incontinence, saddle sensory change, or bowel dysfunction present?
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when back pain, neck pain, weakness, numbness, urinary retention, incontinence, saddle symptoms, or myelopathy terms are present.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Neurologic and bladder symptoms may indicate cord, cauda equina, discitis, epidural collection, or non-MSK neurologic disease.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Positive findings change need for urgent neurologic/spine review, MRI, transfer, and avoiding routine low-back reassurance.
conditionalQuestions.options: Multi-select: weakness / numbness / gait change / urinary retention / incontinence / saddle sensory change / bowel dysfunction / all denied / unsure
conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; neurologic or bladder symptoms are escalation triggers rather than reassurance features.

source.source_section: all-age causes including discitis, epidural collection, and neurologic disease
source.implementation_notes: This add-on should route to pediatric neurologic/spine review when positive.

requiredExam.label: Observe gait
requiredExam.technique: Watch the child stand, take several steps, and return to the caregiver when safe; document refusal, antalgic gait, toe walking, wide-based gait, or inability after analgesia.
requiredExam.findings_options: Comfortable / Antalgic / Refuses to walk / Toe walking / Wide based / Needs support / Unsafe to test
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when the child can be safely observed without forcing painful weight bearing.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Functional weight-bearing status, gait localization, neurologic pattern, and discharge readiness.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available for gait observation; RCH treats acute inability to walk or weight bear as a red flag.
requiredExam.management_change: Inability or unsafe gait changes imaging, observation, senior review, orthopaedic advice, and discharge criteria.
requiredExam.limitations: Do not force painful walking; analgesia, fear, developmental baseline, and fatigue affect interpretation.

source.source_section: key points and observing gait
source.implementation_notes: This is an individual bedside maneuver, not vital signs.

requiredExam.label: Inspect affected limb position
requiredExam.technique: Inspect resting posture, asymmetry, deformity, swelling, erythema, and whether the child protects a hip, knee, ankle, foot, or upper limb.
requiredExam.findings_options: Normal posture / Protective position / Swelling / Erythema / Deformity / Limb held still / Unable to inspect
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform before palpation or range-of-motion testing in all pediatric limp or limb pain concerns.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Joint effusion, dislocation, fracture, inflammatory joint, septic joint posture, or soft-tissue infection.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available for limb-position inspection alone; it localizes the next exam and imaging step.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal position, deformity, swelling, or erythema changes imaging, immobilization, infection workup, and consultation threshold.
requiredExam.limitations: Clothing, pain behavior, developmental baseline, and caregiver handling can obscure findings.

source.source_section: bone and joint examination look findings
source.implementation_notes: Document a single visual inspection maneuver.

requiredExam.label: Palpate focal bony tenderness
requiredExam.technique: Gently palpate the localized bone or metaphyseal area suggested by the history, comparing with the opposite side when tolerated.
requiredExam.findings_options: No focal tenderness / Point tenderness / Metaphyseal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Cannot tolerate / Not localized
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when pain can be localized or injury, fracture, osteomyelitis, or malignancy is possible.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Fracture, osteomyelitis, bone lesion, stress injury, or focal traumatic pain.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available for bony tenderness alone; it guides imaging and infection evaluation.
requiredExam.management_change: Point or metaphyseal tenderness changes site-specific radiography, MRI consideration, labs, and orthopaedic discussion.
requiredExam.limitations: Young children may not localize pain reliably; palpation should be gentle and stopped if severe pain occurs.

source.source_section: diagnosis features including limb pain, swelling, erythema, and metaphyseal point tenderness
source.implementation_notes: Use as a localization maneuver, not a generic MSK exam bundle.

requiredExam.label: Test active joint motion
requiredExam.technique: Ask or encourage the child to move the suspected joint through comfortable active motion or age-appropriate play without forcing movement.
requiredExam.findings_options: Full active motion / Reduced active motion / Pain-limited / Refuses active motion / Pseudoparalysis / Unsafe to test
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when joint pain, swelling, limp, or reduced limb use is present and testing is safe.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Joint inflammation, septic arthritis, fracture pain, pseudoparalysis, or functional limitation.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available for active motion alone; interpretation depends on fever, passive motion, swelling, labs, and imaging.
requiredExam.management_change: Reduced or refused active motion changes urgency of passive motion testing, imaging, aspiration consideration, and admission.
requiredExam.limitations: Fear, pain, developmental stage, and communication ability can limit reliability.

source.source_section: bone and joint examination move findings
source.implementation_notes: Keep active motion separate from passive motion for atomic documentation.

requiredExam.label: Test passive joint motion
requiredExam.technique: Gently move the suspected joint through a small, tolerated passive range in each relevant plane, stopping with pain or resistance.
requiredExam.findings_options: Full passive motion / Pain with passive motion / Reduced passive motion / Guarding / Cannot tolerate / Unsafe to test
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when joint infection, effusion, or inflammatory joint is possible and the child can safely tolerate testing.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Septic arthritis, transient synovitis, joint effusion, inflammatory arthritis, or severe traumatic joint injury.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available for passive motion alone; severe pain with passive motion is an escalation finding interpreted with fever, weight bearing, labs, imaging, and aspiration.
requiredExam.management_change: Painful or reduced passive motion changes orthopaedic consultation, ultrasound or MRI selection, aspiration timing, antibiotics, and admission.
requiredExam.limitations: Do not force a painful joint; analgesia, fear, and guarding can affect interpretation.

source.source_section: bone and joint examination move findings
source.implementation_notes: Do not bundle passive motion with active motion in the displayed maneuver label.

conditionalExam.label: Palpate joint effusion
conditionalExam.technique: Gently palpate the suspected joint for effusion or ballotable swelling, comparing with the opposite side when tolerated.
conditionalExam.findings_options: No effusion / Small effusion / Large effusion / Warm effusion / Cannot tolerate / Not assessed
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Perform when effusion, swollen joint, hip pain, knee pain, septic arthritis, or transient synovitis is suspected.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Joint effusion, septic arthritis, transient synovitis, inflammatory arthritis, or traumatic hemarthrosis.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available for effusion palpation across sites; ultrasound and aspiration may be needed when septic arthritis is possible.
conditionalExam.management_change: Effusion changes ultrasound selection, aspiration consideration, orthopaedic consultation, and admission decisions.
conditionalExam.limitations: Deep hip effusion cannot be reliably palpated; pain and body habitus limit sensitivity.

source.source_section: bone and joint examination feel findings
source.implementation_notes: Use ultrasound when deep hip effusion is suspected.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect open wounds
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect the painful limb and adjacent skin for puncture wounds, draining lesions, cellulitis, bite marks, abrasions, or surgical sites.
conditionalExam.findings_options: No wound / Puncture wound / Abrasion / Bite mark / Draining lesion / Cellulitis / Surgical site / Unable to inspect
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Perform when trauma, puncture, wound, cellulitis, bite, drainage, or infection source is mentioned.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Contiguous infection source, penetrating injury, cellulitis, abscess, bite wound, or surgical-site infection.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available for wound inspection alone; it localizes infection source and changes antibiotic and source-control decisions.
conditionalExam.management_change: Open wound or drainage changes culture/source-control, imaging, tetanus/local wound policy, antibiotic route, and consultation.
conditionalExam.limitations: Clothing, dressings, and pain can limit visualization; do not delay resuscitation for detailed inspection.

source.source_section: look for open wounds or soft tissue infection as potential source
source.implementation_notes: Keep wound inspection as an individual exam maneuver.

conditionalExam.label: Palpate distal pulses
conditionalExam.technique: Palpate distal pulses beyond the injured or painful site and compare with the opposite side; document capillary refill separately only if clinically needed.
conditionalExam.findings_options: Symmetric / Weak / Absent / Delayed refill noted / Cannot assess / Urgent vascular concern
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Perform when fracture, dislocation, severe swelling, numbness, cool limb, pallor, or vascular concern is present.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Vascular compromise after fracture, dislocation, compartment syndrome, or severe swelling.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available for pulse palpation alone; abnormal pulses are limb-threatening escalation findings.
conditionalExam.management_change: Weak or absent pulses change urgency of immobilization, vascular/orthopaedic consultation, transfer, and imaging.
conditionalExam.limitations: Pain, swelling, and child movement can limit palpation; Doppler may be needed locally.

source.source_section: serious pathology and escalation/transfer considerations
source.implementation_notes: Escalate immediately for limb-threatening vascular findings.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect injury pattern
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect visible skin near the painful area and exposed adjacent areas for patterned bruising, bruises of different ages, or locations inconsistent with the history.
conditionalExam.findings_options: No concerning bruising / Patterned bruising / Different ages / Non-mobile location / History mismatch / Unable to inspect
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Perform when bruising, unwitnessed fall, delayed presentation, recurrent injury, or safeguarding concern is present.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Non-accidental injury, occult trauma, bleeding disorder, or injury pattern needing senior review.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available for bruising pattern in this module; use local safeguarding and hematology guidance.
conditionalExam.management_change: Concerning bruising changes child-safety review, documentation, skeletal imaging strategy, hematology testing, and disposition.
conditionalExam.limitations: Full skin examination and safeguarding assessment require local policy, consent/context, and senior review.

source.source_section: differential including child abuse and hematologic causes
source.implementation_notes: Route to local safeguarding guidance when concerning.

redFlags.label: Unable to walk or weight bear
redFlags.action: Treat acute inability to walk or weight bear as a red flag until serious infection, fracture, neurologic, and systemic causes are considered unlikely.
redFlags.rationale: RCH identifies acute inability to walk or weight bear as a red flag in the limping child.
redFlags.diagnostic_target: Septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, fracture, malignancy, neurologic disease, or other serious pathology.
redFlags.management_change: Changes observation, analgesia reassessment, imaging, senior review, consultation, and discharge safety.
redFlags.likelihood_ratio_note: No universal LR is encoded for all causes of non-weight-bearing; source guidance treats it as a high-risk disposition feature.
redFlags.limitations: Pain, fear, developmental stage, and baseline mobility can affect weight-bearing assessment.

source.source_section: key points and discharge criteria
source.implementation_notes: Do not discharge solely on a benign label if the child is not walking comfortably.

redFlags.label: Fever with hot swollen limited joint
redFlags.action: Escalate fever with limp, swelling, warmth, or reduced range of motion for urgent septic arthritis and bone-infection review.
redFlags.rationale: Septic arthritis is an orthopaedic emergency, and bone/joint infection should be suspected with fever, pain, and limited limb or joint use.
redFlags.diagnostic_target: Septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, myositis, or sepsis with musculoskeletal source.
redFlags.management_change: Changes immediate orthopaedic/pediatric consultation, labs, blood cultures, imaging, aspiration, antibiotics, admission, and transfer.
redFlags.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not encoded for this combined red flag; escalation follows guideline-defined danger features and local pathways.
redFlags.limitations: Afebrile presentation does not fully exclude early bone or joint infection.

source.source_section: key points and diagnosis
source.implementation_notes: Antibiotics should not be delayed by surgical planning when urgent treatment is needed.

redFlags.label: Systemically unwell or sepsis physiology
redFlags.action: Activate pediatric sepsis or transfer escalation for shock, toxic appearance, altered mental status, hypoxemia, poor perfusion, or rapidly worsening illness.
redFlags.rationale: Recognition and management of the seriously unwell or septic child is the most urgent consideration in bone and joint infection.
redFlags.diagnostic_target: Sepsis, shock, disseminated infection, metastatic infection, or life-threatening systemic illness.
redFlags.management_change: Changes resuscitation priority, cultures, antibiotics, fluids, senior review, transfer, and whether routine workup can wait.
redFlags.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not assigned to sepsis physiology as a single test; use local pediatric sepsis criteria and source guidance.
redFlags.limitations: Children can compensate until late; absence of hypotension does not remove concern when other danger signs are present.

source.source_section: management and seriously unwell or septic child consideration
source.implementation_notes: Pair with pediatric sepsis pathway when abnormal physiology is present.

redFlags.label: Night pain weight loss pallor bruising
redFlags.action: Escalate persistent limp with night pain, weight loss, pallor, bruising, bleeding, or recurrent fevers for systemic, hematologic, inflammatory, or malignant causes.
redFlags.rationale: Limp can be caused by malignancy, hematologic disease, rheumatologic disease, or chronic infection, not only sprain or transient synovitis.
redFlags.diagnostic_target: Leukemia, bone tumor, inflammatory arthritis, vasculitis, hemophilia, sickle-cell disease, or chronic infection.
redFlags.management_change: Changes CBC and film, inflammatory markers, admission, specialty consultation, and outpatient safety-netting.
redFlags.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not encoded for this broad systemic-red-flag cluster; it is an anti-anchoring trigger.
redFlags.limitations: A normal initial exam does not remove concern if systemic symptoms persist or worsen.

source.source_section: differential diagnosis including malignancy, rheumatologic, and hematologic causes
source.implementation_notes: Use as a routing guard for hematology/oncology/rheumatology consideration.

redFlags.label: Safeguarding concern or inconsistent injury
redFlags.action: Escalate injury that is unwitnessed, developmentally implausible, delayed in presentation, recurrent, or inconsistent with exam findings.
redFlags.rationale: Inflicted injury is part of the pediatric limp differential and needs senior review rather than a routine sprain label.
redFlags.diagnostic_target: Non-accidental injury, occult fracture, bleeding disorder, or unsafe discharge environment.
redFlags.management_change: Changes child-safety review, documentation, imaging plan, admission/disposition, and PHI-safe audit handling.
redFlags.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not assigned to safeguarding concern; local child protection policy and multidisciplinary review determine next steps.
redFlags.limitations: This module flags concern but does not replace jurisdiction-specific safeguarding protocols.

source.source_section: differential diagnosis including child abuse
source.implementation_notes: Use local safeguarding policy and do not export PHI-rich narrative.

initialTests.label: No routine tests for low-risk comfortable limp
initialTests.action: Avoid routine investigations when the child is systemically well, afebrile, walking or using the limb without significant discomfort, and high-risk diagnoses are unlikely.
initialTests.rationale: RCH states most children with limp do not require investigation and bone/joint infection testing may be unnecessary in a well, afebrile, comfortable child.
initialTests.diagnostic_target: Low-risk transient synovitis, acute myositis, minor sprain, or resolving minor trauma after danger features are absent.
initialTests.management_change: Changes workup toward analgesia, observation, discharge criteria, and follow-up instead of unnecessary labs or imaging.
initialTests.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to testing restraint; it depends on source-defined low-risk conditions and follow-up reliability.
initialTests.limitations: Do not apply if the child is febrile, systemically unwell, unable to bear weight, has severe pain, or has unclear follow-up.

source.source_section: key points, investigations, and discharge criteria
source.implementation_notes: Testing restraint must be paired with documented low-risk criteria.

initialTests.label: X-ray localized pain or injury area
initialTests.action: Order radiography of the localized area of concern when pain, trauma, deformity, focal tenderness, or persistent inability to bear weight localizes a fracture or bone lesion concern.
initialTests.rationale: ACR rates radiography of the localized lower-extremity area as usually appropriate in a child up to age 5 with localized acute limp and no infection concern; RCH uses X-ray to exclude fracture or tumor.
initialTests.diagnostic_target: Fracture, dislocation, bone lesion, slipped hip pathology, or tumor causing localized pediatric limp.
initialTests.management_change: Positive or concerning imaging changes immobilization, orthopaedic referral, activity restriction, admission, or specialty workup.
initialTests.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not encoded because performance varies by site, fracture type, timing, and radiographic view; source guidance provides appropriateness rather than a single diagnostic LR.
initialTests.limitations: Early osteomyelitis X-rays may be normal and occult fractures may need follow-up imaging or MRI when symptoms persist.

source.source_section: variant 2 localized symptoms without infection and RCH X-ray comments
source.implementation_notes: Use site-specific imaging and minimize radiation.

initialTests.label: Infection labs and blood culture route
initialTests.action: When systemic illness, significant bone or joint pain, fever, or limitation of movement is present, obtain FBC, ESR, CRP, and blood cultures before antibiotics when feasible.
initialTests.rationale: RCH recommends urgent investigation and orthopaedic discussion for systemic illness or significant bone/joint pain and notes inflammatory markers and cultures support diagnosis and monitoring.
initialTests.diagnostic_target: Osteomyelitis, septic arthritis, bacteremia, malignancy mimic, or inflammatory disease.
initialTests.management_change: Results change antibiotic route, admission, imaging, aspiration timing, response monitoring, and specialist consultation.
initialTests.likelihood_ratio_note: Guidelines state no laboratory test or combination is specific for bone or joint infection; use results with exam, culture, imaging, and trajectory.
initialTests.limitations: Inflammatory markers may be normal early, in chronic infection, or in small-bone infection.

source.source_section: investigations and pathology
source.implementation_notes: Do not treat normal labs as definitive exclusion in early infection.

initialTests.label: Ultrasound hip effusion route
initialTests.action: Use ultrasound to identify joint effusion when septic arthritis of the hip or another effusion-based decision is suspected, while recognizing it is not specific.
initialTests.rationale: RCH notes ultrasound can identify effusion in suspected septic arthritis and is highly sensitive but not specific; ACR rates hip ultrasound usually appropriate when hip infection is suspected.
initialTests.diagnostic_target: Hip effusion, septic arthritis, transient synovitis, or effusion needing aspiration decision.
initialTests.management_change: Effusion changes orthopaedic consultation, aspiration, MRI, antibiotics, observation, and admission decisions.
initialTests.likelihood_ratio_note: RCH describes ultrasound as highly sensitive but not specific; no single LR is encoded for all pediatric hip-effusion contexts.
initialTests.limitations: Ultrasound does not reliably distinguish septic arthritis from transient synovitis and can be falsely negative.

source.source_section: imaging investigations and ultrasound
source.implementation_notes: Use ultrasound as an effusion test, not as a stand-alone rule-out for septic arthritis.

initialTests.label: MRI for osteomyelitis localization
initialTests.action: Use MRI when osteomyelitis, deep infection, abscess, marrow involvement, myositis, or infection localization remains a concern after clinical assessment.
initialTests.rationale: RCH describes MRI with contrast as preferred for osteomyelitis where symptoms are localized and ACR rates MRI usually appropriate in several infection-concern limp variants.
initialTests.diagnostic_target: Osteomyelitis, subperiosteal abscess, marrow involvement, myositis, deep infection, or occult serious pathology.
initialTests.management_change: MRI findings change antibiotic duration/route, surgical drainage, infectious diseases review, orthopaedic plan, and transfer.
initialTests.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not encoded because MRI performance varies by site, timing, contrast protocol, and reference standard; use source-defined imaging indications.
initialTests.limitations: Access, sedation needs, contrast policy, and local MRI availability can delay imaging; do not delay urgent antibiotics in septic or unwell children.

source.source_section: imaging investigations and MRI
source.implementation_notes: Use with local imaging access and sedation policy.

dispositionRules.label: Immediate orthopedic route for septic arthritis
dispositionRules.action: Discuss suspected septic arthritis urgently with orthopaedics and pediatrics; aspiration, washout, and antibiotics are time-critical and antibiotics should not be delayed when urgent treatment is needed.
dispositionRules.rationale: RCH states septic arthritis is an orthopaedic emergency and urgent surgical intervention should not delay antibiotics.
dispositionRules.diagnostic_target: Septic arthritis or joint infection needing aspiration, arthrotomy/washout, antibiotics, and admission.
dispositionRules.management_change: Changes immediate consultation, antibiotics, operative planning, admission, transfer, and monitoring.
dispositionRules.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to this management rule; urgency is guideline-defined.
dispositionRules.limitations: Local antimicrobial choice and operative timing depend on local orthopaedic, pediatric, and infectious diseases guidance.

source.source_section: treatment and consultation
source.implementation_notes: Do not let documentation delay urgent treatment.

dispositionRules.label: Bone infection admission and antibiotic route
dispositionRules.action: For suspected osteomyelitis or bone/joint infection, coordinate pediatrics, orthopaedics, cultures when feasible, IV antibiotics, imaging, and admission or transfer according to local capability.
dispositionRules.rationale: Bone and joint infections can be life-threatening and cause long-term disability, and suspected cases require urgent investigation and specialist discussion.
dispositionRules.diagnostic_target: Osteomyelitis, deep bone infection, abscess, myositis, septic arthritis, or disseminated infection.
dispositionRules.management_change: Changes admission, antibiotic timing, imaging, surgical drainage consideration, ID consult, and monitoring of fever and inflammatory markers.
dispositionRules.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to this management route; use guideline criteria, local antimicrobial policy, and clinical trajectory.
dispositionRules.limitations: Antibiotic selection, duration, and oral switch criteria vary by organism, resistance, age, site, and local guideline.

source.source_section: purpose, diagnosis, treatment principles, and governance
source.implementation_notes: Use local antimicrobial stewardship guidance.

dispositionRules.label: Low-risk discharge only when walking comfortably
dispositionRules.action: Discharge only when the cause has been identified and managed or the child is ambulating comfortably with or without analgesia, high-risk diagnoses are unlikely, and follow-up is in place.
dispositionRules.rationale: RCH discharge criteria require comfortable ambulation, low likelihood of high-risk diagnoses, and a follow-up plan.
dispositionRules.diagnostic_target: Low-risk limp, transient synovitis, resolving minor trauma, or mild sprain after danger features are absent.
dispositionRules.management_change: Changes discharge readiness, return precautions, follow-up timing, analgesia plan, and testing restraint.
dispositionRules.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to discharge criteria; it depends on source-defined safety conditions and reliable follow-up.
dispositionRules.limitations: Do not use for persistent non-weight-bearing, fever, unclear diagnosis, unreliable follow-up, or systemic red flags.

source.source_section: consider discharge when
source.implementation_notes: Make the discharge conditions visible in concise copy/export.

dispositionRules.label: Persistent limp or negative X-ray route
dispositionRules.action: Seek orthopaedic or pediatric advice when X-ray is normal but the limp persists, the child cannot bear weight, or symptoms worsen despite analgesia and follow-up.
dispositionRules.rationale: CHQ lists X-ray with no abnormality but persistent limp and inability to walk due to inability to weight bear among non-critical children who may need advice or referral.
dispositionRules.diagnostic_target: Occult fracture, infection, malignancy, hip pathology, inflammatory disease, or persistent serious pathology after initial imaging.
dispositionRules.management_change: Changes follow-up interval, repeat imaging, MRI, immobilization, orthopaedic referral, admission, or transfer.
dispositionRules.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to persistent-limp routing; it is a safety-net rule after initial evaluation.
dispositionRules.limitations: Persistence thresholds depend on age, symptom severity, ability to follow up, and local referral access.

source.source_section: escalation and advice outside ED
source.implementation_notes: Use when initial imaging is unrevealing but function remains abnormal.

dispositionRules.label: Referred or systemic cause route
dispositionRules.action: Route to pediatric abdominal, GU, neuro, hematology, rheumatology, oncology, or safeguarding review when symptoms extend beyond an isolated limp or injury.
dispositionRules.rationale: CHQ and RCH list neurologic, intra-abdominal, GU, hematologic, rheumatologic, malignancy, and child-abuse causes of limp.
dispositionRules.diagnostic_target: Appendicitis, torsion, UTI, neurologic disease, leukemia, bone tumor, inflammatory arthritis, vasculitis, hemophilia, sickle-cell disease, or non-accidental injury.
dispositionRules.management_change: Changes secondary validated workup selection, labs, imaging, specialty consultation, admission, and audit traceability.
dispositionRules.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to cross-specialty routing; use source-defined symptom domains and validated secondary workups.
dispositionRules.limitations: This module flags routing needs but does not replace the disease-specific validated workup once the alternate source dominates.

source.source_section: genitourinary, intraabdominal, neurological, rheumatologic, and hematologic causes
source.implementation_notes: Keep unsupported free text blocked unless a validated secondary intent is selected.

decisionTrees.label: Treat acute inability to walk or weight bear as a pediatric limp red flag.

source.source_section: Pediatric limp, hot joint, injury, or non-weight bearing derived decision tree: triage and escalation
source.implementation_notes: Do not discharge solely on a benign label if the child is not walking comfortably.

decisionTrees.action: Treat acute inability to walk or weight bear as a red flag until serious infection, fracture, neurologic disease, systemic disease, or unsafe discharge has been addressed.
decisionTrees.rationale: RCH identifies acute inability to walk or weight bear as a red flag in a limping child and links discharge readiness to comfortable ambulation with high-risk diagnoses unlikely.
decisionTrees.diagnostic_target: Septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, fracture, malignancy, neurologic disease, or other serious pathology causing non-weight-bearing.
decisionTrees.management_change: Changes observation, analgesia reassessment, targeted imaging/labs, senior review, consultation, and discharge safety.
decisionTrees.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag management row; route testing, treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition using the cited pediatric limp guidance.
decisionTrees.limitations: Pain, fear, developmental stage, baseline mobility, analgesia timing, and local follow-up access can affect weight-bearing assessment.

decisionTrees.label: Choose pediatric limp testing by branch: no routine tests only for low-risk comfortable limp; X-ray for localized injury; FBE/ESR/CRP and blood culture for infection concern; ultrasound for hip effusion concern.

source.source_section: Pediatric limp, hot joint, injury, or non-weight bearing derived decision tree: initial tests
source.implementation_notes: Testing restraint must be paired with documented low-risk criteria.

decisionTrees.action: Avoid routine investigations only when the child is low risk and comfortable; otherwise select site-specific X-ray for localized trauma or bone lesion concern, FBE/ESR/CRP and blood culture before antibiotics when infection is possible, hip ultrasound when effusion will change aspiration or orthopaedic decisions, and MRI when osteomyelitis or deep infection remains possible.
decisionTrees.rationale: RCH and ACR guidance route pediatric limp testing by red flags, localization, infection concern, and discharge safety rather than a universal test panel.
decisionTrees.diagnostic_target: Low-risk transient synovitis or minor injury versus fracture, septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, malignancy, hip disease, or other serious pathology.
decisionTrees.management_change: Changes testing restraint, imaging site, culture timing, MRI need, aspiration/orthopaedic discussion, admission, and follow-up.
decisionTrees.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this testing-selection row because test performance depends on site, timing, suspected diagnosis, and prior treatment.
decisionTrees.limitations: Normal early X-ray, normal inflammatory markers, or absent fever do not reliably exclude early bone/joint infection when clinical concern persists.

decisionTrees.label: Keep septic arthritis or osteomyelitis in the differential until red flags and objective findings make bone/joint infection unlikely.

source.source_section: Pediatric limp, hot joint, injury, or non-weight bearing derived decision tree: diagnostic branching
source.implementation_notes: Use local pediatric sepsis and orthopaedic pathways immediately when the child is unwell.

decisionTrees.action: For fever, hot/swollen joint, reduced range of motion, limited limb use, severe localized bone pain, or systemic illness, keep septic arthritis and osteomyelitis active until orthopaedic/pediatric review, cultures/labs, aspiration or imaging, and clinical trajectory make infection unlikely.
decisionTrees.rationale: RCH bone/joint infection guidance emphasizes that septic arthritis is an orthopaedic emergency and bone/joint infection can cause life-threatening illness and long-term disability.
decisionTrees.diagnostic_target: Septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, myositis, discitis, abscess, bacteremia, or deep soft-tissue infection.
decisionTrees.management_change: Changes urgency of cultures, inflammatory markers, aspiration, MRI, antibiotics, admission, surgery, transfer, and monitoring.
decisionTrees.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this broad infection frame; use fever, weight-bearing, ROM, inflammatory markers, cultures, aspiration, imaging, and trajectory together.
decisionTrees.limitations: Afebrile presentation, early normal X-ray, and normal inflammatory markers can occur early or in selected bone/joint infections.

decisionTrees.label: Escalate suspected septic arthritis for urgent orthopaedic review, aspiration or washout planning, cultures, and antibiotics.

source.source_section: Pediatric limp, hot joint, injury, or non-weight bearing derived decision tree: management routing
source.implementation_notes: Do not let documentation delay urgent treatment.

decisionTrees.action: Discuss suspected septic arthritis urgently with orthopaedics and pediatrics, obtain blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, arrange aspiration with Gram stain/culture where appropriate, prepare for washout if indicated, start empiric IV antibiotics when urgent treatment is needed, and admit or transfer for pediatric orthopaedic care.
decisionTrees.rationale: RCH states septic arthritis is an orthopaedic emergency and that urgent surgical intervention should not delay antibiotics when urgent treatment is needed.
decisionTrees.diagnostic_target: Septic arthritis or joint infection requiring aspiration, arthrotomy/washout, IV antibiotics, admission, or transfer.
decisionTrees.management_change: Changes immediate consultation, cultures, aspiration, operative planning, antibiotic timing, admission, transfer, and monitoring.
decisionTrees.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this emergency management rule; the disposition is driven by guideline-defined septic arthritis concern.
decisionTrees.limitations: Local antibiotic selection, aspiration access, operative timing, and transfer destination require local orthopaedic, pediatric, and infectious diseases policy.

treatmentOptions.label: Escalate acute inability to walk or weight bear until serious infection, fracture, neurologic disease, systemic disease, or unsafe discharge is unlikely.

source.source_section: Pediatric limp, hot joint, injury, or non-weight bearing derived treatment options: urgent care
source.implementation_notes: Do not discharge solely on a benign label if the child is not walking comfortably.

treatmentOptions.action: Provide analgesia, reassess weight bearing, obtain senior review, and route to infection, fracture, neurologic, systemic, safeguarding, admission, or transfer pathway when the child cannot walk or bear weight.
treatmentOptions.rationale: Acute inability to walk or weight bear is a pediatric limp red flag and should not be discharged as benign until high-risk causes and follow-up safety are addressed.
treatmentOptions.diagnostic_target: Septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, fracture, malignancy, neurologic disease, systemic illness, or unsafe discharge context.
treatmentOptions.management_change: Changes analgesia reassessment, imaging/lab selection, consultation, admission, transfer, and discharge safety.
treatmentOptions.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag treatment row; use the finding to route testing, consultation, monitoring, and disposition.
treatmentOptions.limitations: Pain, fear, developmental baseline, analgesia timing, and family follow-up access can change apparent weight-bearing ability.

treatmentOptions.label: Treat suspected septic arthritis as an orthopaedic emergency requiring urgent review, aspiration or washout planning, cultures, and IV antibiotics.

source.source_section: Pediatric limp, hot joint, injury, or non-weight bearing derived treatment options: high-risk pathway
source.implementation_notes: Do not let documentation delay urgent treatment.

treatmentOptions.action: Discuss immediately with orthopaedics and pediatrics, obtain cultures when feasible, arrange aspiration or operative washout when indicated, start empiric IV antibiotics when urgent treatment is needed, and admit or transfer.
treatmentOptions.rationale: Septic arthritis is an orthopaedic emergency; early orthopaedic intervention and antibiotics reduce risk of joint damage and systemic complications.
treatmentOptions.diagnostic_target: Septic arthritis or infected joint requiring urgent aspiration, washout, IV antibiotics, admission, or transfer.
treatmentOptions.management_change: Changes immediate consultation, procedural planning, antibiotic timing, admission, transfer, and reassessment.
treatmentOptions.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this emergency treatment row; suspicion of septic arthritis determines urgency.
treatmentOptions.limitations: Local organism risk, antimicrobial policy, operating-room access, and transfer capability determine exact treatment sequence.

treatmentOptions.label: Admit suspected bone or joint infection for cultures, imaging, IV antibiotics, and orthopaedic or infectious diseases management.

source.source_section: Pediatric limp, hot joint, injury, or non-weight bearing derived treatment options: lower-risk pathway
source.implementation_notes: Use local antimicrobial stewardship guidance.

treatmentOptions.action: Coordinate pediatric and orthopaedic admission or transfer, obtain cultures before antibiotics when feasible, use X-ray to evaluate fracture or tumor while recognizing early osteomyelitis may be normal, obtain MRI when localized osteomyelitis or deep infection remains possible, start IV antibiotics under local antimicrobial guidance, and involve infectious diseases for complex cases.
treatmentOptions.rationale: Pediatric bone and joint infection can be life-threatening and can cause long-term disability; suspected cases require urgent investigation and specialist discussion.
treatmentOptions.diagnostic_target: Osteomyelitis, septic arthritis, myositis, discitis, abscess, bacteremia, or complicated bone/joint infection.
treatmentOptions.management_change: Changes admission, culture timing, imaging, IV antibiotic route, drainage/surgery consideration, ID review, transfer, and monitoring.
treatmentOptions.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management route; use clinical features, cultures, inflammatory markers, imaging, and trajectory together.
treatmentOptions.limitations: Antibiotic choice, admission unit, imaging access, oral switch criteria, and total duration depend on organism, site, resistance risk, host factors, and local guideline.

treatmentOptions.label: Reassess pain, weight-bearing, fever, range of motion, neurovascular status, CRP/ESR/WBC, cultures, imaging results, and follow-up readiness.

source.source_section: Pediatric limp, hot joint, injury, or non-weight bearing derived treatment options: monitoring
source.implementation_notes: Testing restraint must be paired with documented low-risk criteria.

treatmentOptions.action: Repeat vital signs, pain and walking assessment, joint range of motion, neurovascular exam, culture review, CRP/ESR/WBC trend when infection is treated, imaging follow-up, antibiotic tolerance, oral switch readiness, and family return precautions before discharge or de-escalation.
treatmentOptions.rationale: Pediatric limp and bone/joint infection disposition depends on reassessment of weight-bearing, fever, objective results, treatment response, and reliable follow-up.
treatmentOptions.diagnostic_target: Worsening infection, occult fracture, persistent hip disease, systemic mimic, treatment failure, oral-switch readiness, or safe discharge readiness.
treatmentOptions.management_change: Changes escalation, repeat imaging/labs, antibiotic route, admission duration, specialty follow-up, and safety-net instructions.
treatmentOptions.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this monitoring row; deterioration or discordant objective findings should reopen the diagnostic branch.
treatmentOptions.limitations: Follow-up safety depends on family access, pending result ownership, local specialty availability, and ability to return promptly.

source_metadata.citation_text: Caird MS, Flynn JM, Leung YL, Millman JE, D'Italia JG, Dormans JP. Factors distinguishing septic arthritis from transient synovitis of the hip in children. A prospective study. J Bone Joint Surg Am. 2006;88(6):1251-1257.

provenance.source_material: Royal Children's Hospital limp and bone/joint infection guidelines, Children's Health Queensland 2026 limp and bone/joint infection guidance, ACR acutely limping child appropriateness criteria, Kocher septic hip prediction study, Caird septic hip CRP study, and local module evidence rows
provenance.review_note: Pediatric limp/hot-joint tree is threshold-cited and patient-traversable; local pediatric sepsis criteria, antimicrobial formulary, orthopaedic washout timing, imaging/sedation access, safeguarding policy, and transfer thresholds require clinician governance.
