root.description: Activate for child or adolescent with limp, refusal to walk, non-weight-bearing, hot/swollen joint, suspected septic arthritis or osteomyelitis, limb pain, localized injury, hip/groin/thigh/knee pain, persistent limp, or clinician-chosen pediatric MSK evaluation.

endpoint_missing_context.description: Route here when pediatric limp or hot-joint management cannot be classified because core history, exam, vitals, objective results, comorbidity, or follow-up data are unavailable.

pediatric_msk_limp_hot_joint_initial_ass.description: Initial pediatric limp assessment requires age/development, duration, injury and safeguarding history, weight-bearing after analgesia, fever/vitals, focused MSK exam, infection/fracture/hip/systemic screen, targeted testing, and follow-up constraints.

criteria_options.label: Low-risk limp: no investigations only if no red flags, mild/no discomfort with analgesia, and review plan within 7 days of onset
criteria_options.criteria_text: No investigations are indicated only when history/exam red flags are absent, the child ambulates with mild or no discomfort after simple analgesia, and a review plan exists within 7 days of limp onset if symptoms persist.
data_needed: ability to weight bear after analgesia

criteria_options.label: Consult for limp lasting >7 days, inability to weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, systemic illness, malignancy features, or septic arthritis concern
criteria_options.criteria_text: Consult paediatrics/orthopaedics when symptoms last more than 7 days, the child cannot weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, is systemically unwell, has malignancy features, or septic arthritis is suspected.

criteria_options.label: Hip septic arthritis support: CRP >=20 mg/L and ultrasound effusion >=7 mm increase specificity/sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH cites evidence that CRP >=20 mg/L and hip ultrasound effusion >=7 mm has high specificity and sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis; use with clinical suspicion and orthopaedic consultation.

criteria_options.label: Kocher septic hip predictors: fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR >=40 mm/hr, WBC >12.0 x10^9/L; 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 predictors correspond to <0.2%, 3.0%, 40.0%, 93.1%, 99.6% predicted septic arthritis
criteria_options.criteria_text: Use Kocher predictors only as hip septic-arthritis risk support, not as discharge permission: history/temperature fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR at least 40 mm/hr, and WBC more than 12.0 x10^9/L; predicted septic arthritis rises from <0.2% with zero predictors to 99.6% with all four predictors.
criteria_options.source_section: Independent multivariate predictors and probability algorithm

criteria_options.label: Caird septic hip support: oral temperature >38.5 C and CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) increase concern; 3, 4, and 5 predictors correspond to 83%, 93%, and 98% septic arthritis probability in aspirated high-suspicion children
criteria_options.criteria_text: Caird prospective hip aspiration study found fever, CRP, ESR, refusal to bear weight, and WBC helpful in high-suspicion children; CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) was a strong independent risk factor, and 3, 4, or 5 predictors gave 83%, 93%, and 98% probabilities.

criteria_options.label: Transient synovitis support: low-grade temperature <38.5 C, able to walk or weight bear with mild pain, serious causes excluded, rest/NSAIDs, and recovery expected over days to weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ describes transient synovitis as an exclusion diagnosis in a child with low-grade temperature <38.5 C who is usually able to walk or weight bear with mild pain; use rest/NSAIDs and safety-net when serious infection, SUFE, Perthes, fracture, and systemic disease are unlikely.

criteria_options.label: Age-specific hip disease: SUFE risk increases with weight >90th percentile and about 20% bilateral disease; Perthes concern is typical in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ highlights SUFE risk with weight >90th percentile, endocrine/puberty risk, and about 20% bilateral involvement requiring non-weight-bearing and prompt operative treatment; Perthes is considered especially in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp and may have normal early X-ray.
criteria_options.source_section: Slipped upper femoral epiphysis and Perthes disease

criteria_options.label: Bone/joint infection testing: FBE, ESR, CRP, and blood culture before antibiotics when feasible; early osteomyelitis X-ray can stay normal for 7-10 days; MRI with contrast is preferred when localized osteomyelitis remains possible
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH bone/joint infection guidance recommends FBE, ESR, CRP, blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, joint aspiration when appropriate without delaying antibiotics, X-ray recognizing early osteomyelitis may be normal for 7-10 days, ultrasound for effusion but not specificity, and MRI with contrast as preferred localized osteomyelitis imaging.

criteria_options.label: RCH bone/joint infection treatment: cefazolin 50 mg/kg max 2 g IV q8h; switch to cefalexin 45 mg/kg max 1.5 g q8h after 2-3 days IV when improving, afebrile, and markers improve; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH lists cefazolin 50 mg/kg (maximum 2 g) IV every 8 hours for uncomplicated bone/joint infection, oral cefalexin 45 mg/kg (maximum 1.5 g) every 8 hours, oral switch after 2-3 days IV if clinical improvement, afebrile, and improving inflammatory markers, and total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis or 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.

criteria_options.label: CHQ bone/joint infection oral switch/stopping: IV minimum 48 hours; afebrile at least 24 hours; CRP <20 mg/L or down >2/3 from peak; recheck CRP 1 week after oral switch and before stopping; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ recommends initial IV therapy for a minimum of 48 hours, oral switch when uncomplicated disease is clinically improved, afebrile at least 24 hours, tolerating oral intake, and CRP <20 mg/L or down by more than two-thirds from peak; recheck CRP one week after oral switch and before stopping, with total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis and 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.
data_needed: CRP one week after oral switch
criteria_options.source_section: IV-to-oral switch, monitoring, and treatment duration

criteria_options.label: Complicated bone/joint infection review: ID/AMS code within 24-48 hours for MRSA/complex disease; seek senior ID before IV beyond 2-3 days, PICC, neonate, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ requires ID review and antimicrobial stewardship authorization within 24-48 hours for MRSA or complex therapy contexts and senior ID input before longer-term IV/PICC plans beyond 2-3 days or for complex disease, neonates, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia.
criteria_options.source_section: Complex therapy, AMS authorization, and ID review

criteria_options.label: Occult toddler fracture support: negative initial radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture; follow-up radiographs found toddler fracture in 16 of 39 (41%) clinically presumed cases
criteria_options.criteria_text: ACR notes negative radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture and cites a series where 16 of 39 clinically presumed toddler fractures with negative initial imaging were seen on follow-up radiographs.
criteria_options.source_section: Radiography and occult toddler fracture discussion

criteria_options.label: CHQ limp safety-net: return for fever, worsening/nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, persistence >14 days, or tired/lethargic/unwell state
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ parent advice recommends re-presentation for fever, worsening or nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, symptoms persisting more than 14 days, or if the child is tired, lethargic, or unwell.

criteria_options.label: X-ray localized pain or injury area
criteria_options.criteria_text: X-ray localized pain or injury area Order radiography of the localized area of concern when pain, trauma, deformity, focal tenderness, or persistent inability to bear weight localizes a fracture or bone lesion concern. Positive or concerning imaging changes immobilization, orthopaedic referral, activity restriction, admission, or specialty workup. Fracture, dislocation, bone lesion, slipped hip pathology, or tumor causing localized pediatric limp. ACR rates radiography of the localized lower-extremity area as usually appropriate in a child up to age 5 with localized acute limp and no infection concern; RCH uses X-ray to exclude fracture or tumor.
criteria_options.source_section: variant 2 localized symptoms without infection and RCH X-ray comments

criteria_options.label: Caird septic hip support: oral temperature >38.5 C and CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) increase concern; 3, 4, and 5 predictors correspond to 83%, 93%, and 98% septic arthritis probability in aspirated high-suspicion children
criteria_options.criteria_text: Caird prospective hip aspiration study found fever, CRP, ESR, refusal to bear weight, and WBC helpful in high-suspicion children; CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) was a strong independent risk factor, and 3, 4, or 5 predictors gave 83%, 93%, and 98% probabilities.

criteria_options.label: Transient synovitis support: low-grade temperature <38.5 C, able to walk or weight bear with mild pain, serious causes excluded, rest/NSAIDs, and recovery expected over days to weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ describes transient synovitis as an exclusion diagnosis in a child with low-grade temperature <38.5 C who is usually able to walk or weight bear with mild pain; use rest/NSAIDs and safety-net when serious infection, SUFE, Perthes, fracture, and systemic disease are unlikely.

criteria_options.label: Age-specific hip disease: SUFE risk increases with weight >90th percentile and about 20% bilateral disease; Perthes concern is typical in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ highlights SUFE risk with weight >90th percentile, endocrine/puberty risk, and about 20% bilateral involvement requiring non-weight-bearing and prompt operative treatment; Perthes is considered especially in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp and may have normal early X-ray.
criteria_options.source_section: Slipped upper femoral epiphysis and Perthes disease

criteria_options.label: Bone/joint infection testing: FBE, ESR, CRP, and blood culture before antibiotics when feasible; early osteomyelitis X-ray can stay normal for 7-10 days; MRI with contrast is preferred when localized osteomyelitis remains possible
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH bone/joint infection guidance recommends FBE, ESR, CRP, blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, joint aspiration when appropriate without delaying antibiotics, X-ray recognizing early osteomyelitis may be normal for 7-10 days, ultrasound for effusion but not specificity, and MRI with contrast as preferred localized osteomyelitis imaging.

criteria_options.label: RCH bone/joint infection treatment: cefazolin 50 mg/kg max 2 g IV q8h; switch to cefalexin 45 mg/kg max 1.5 g q8h after 2-3 days IV when improving, afebrile, and markers improve; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH lists cefazolin 50 mg/kg (maximum 2 g) IV every 8 hours for uncomplicated bone/joint infection, oral cefalexin 45 mg/kg (maximum 1.5 g) every 8 hours, oral switch after 2-3 days IV if clinical improvement, afebrile, and improving inflammatory markers, and total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis or 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.

criteria_options.label: CHQ bone/joint infection oral switch/stopping: IV minimum 48 hours; afebrile at least 24 hours; CRP <20 mg/L or down >2/3 from peak; recheck CRP 1 week after oral switch and before stopping; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ recommends initial IV therapy for a minimum of 48 hours, oral switch when uncomplicated disease is clinically improved, afebrile at least 24 hours, tolerating oral intake, and CRP <20 mg/L or down by more than two-thirds from peak; recheck CRP one week after oral switch and before stopping, with total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis and 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.
data_needed: CRP one week after oral switch
criteria_options.source_section: IV-to-oral switch, monitoring, and treatment duration

criteria_options.label: Complicated bone/joint infection review: ID/AMS code within 24-48 hours for MRSA/complex disease; seek senior ID before IV beyond 2-3 days, PICC, neonate, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ requires ID review and antimicrobial stewardship authorization within 24-48 hours for MRSA or complex therapy contexts and senior ID input before longer-term IV/PICC plans beyond 2-3 days or for complex disease, neonates, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia.
criteria_options.source_section: Complex therapy, AMS authorization, and ID review

criteria_options.label: Occult toddler fracture support: negative initial radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture; follow-up radiographs found toddler fracture in 16 of 39 (41%) clinically presumed cases
criteria_options.criteria_text: ACR notes negative radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture and cites a series where 16 of 39 clinically presumed toddler fractures with negative initial imaging were seen on follow-up radiographs.
criteria_options.source_section: Radiography and occult toddler fracture discussion

criteria_options.label: CHQ limp safety-net: return for fever, worsening/nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, persistence >14 days, or tired/lethargic/unwell state
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ parent advice recommends re-presentation for fever, worsening or nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, symptoms persisting more than 14 days, or if the child is tired, lethargic, or unwell.

criteria_options.label: X-ray localized pain or injury area
criteria_options.criteria_text: X-ray localized pain or injury area Order radiography of the localized area of concern when pain, trauma, deformity, focal tenderness, or persistent inability to bear weight localizes a fracture or bone lesion concern. Positive or concerning imaging changes immobilization, orthopaedic referral, activity restriction, admission, or specialty workup. Fracture, dislocation, bone lesion, slipped hip pathology, or tumor causing localized pediatric limp. ACR rates radiography of the localized lower-extremity area as usually appropriate in a child up to age 5 with localized acute limp and no infection concern; RCH uses X-ray to exclude fracture or tumor.
criteria_options.source_section: variant 2 localized symptoms without infection and RCH X-ray comments

pediatric_msk_limp_hot_joint_septic_arth.description: Use when fever, non-weight-bearing, joint restriction, inflammatory markers, effusion, aspiration findings, or clinician concern make septic arthritis possible.

criteria_options.label: Low-risk limp: no investigations only if no red flags, mild/no discomfort with analgesia, and review plan within 7 days of onset
criteria_options.criteria_text: No investigations are indicated only when history/exam red flags are absent, the child ambulates with mild or no discomfort after simple analgesia, and a review plan exists within 7 days of limp onset if symptoms persist.
data_needed: ability to weight bear after analgesia

criteria_options.label: Consult for limp lasting >7 days, inability to weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, systemic illness, malignancy features, or septic arthritis concern
criteria_options.criteria_text: Consult paediatrics/orthopaedics when symptoms last more than 7 days, the child cannot weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, is systemically unwell, has malignancy features, or septic arthritis is suspected.

criteria_options.criteria_text: Consult paediatrics/orthopaedics when symptoms last more than 7 days, the child cannot weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, is systemically unwell, has malignancy features, or septic arthritis is suspected.

criteria_options.label: Hip septic arthritis support: CRP >=20 mg/L and ultrasound effusion >=7 mm increase specificity/sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH cites evidence that CRP >=20 mg/L and hip ultrasound effusion >=7 mm has high specificity and sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis; use with clinical suspicion and orthopaedic consultation.

criteria_options.label: Kocher septic hip predictors: fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR >=40 mm/hr, WBC >12.0 x10^9/L; 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 predictors correspond to <0.2%, 3.0%, 40.0%, 93.1%, 99.6% predicted septic arthritis
criteria_options.criteria_text: Use Kocher predictors only as hip septic-arthritis risk support, not as discharge permission: history/temperature fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR at least 40 mm/hr, and WBC more than 12.0 x10^9/L; predicted septic arthritis rises from <0.2% with zero predictors to 99.6% with all four predictors.
criteria_options.source_section: Independent multivariate predictors and probability algorithm

criteria_options.label: Caird septic hip support: oral temperature >38.5 C and CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) increase concern; 3, 4, and 5 predictors correspond to 83%, 93%, and 98% septic arthritis probability in aspirated high-suspicion children
criteria_options.criteria_text: Caird prospective hip aspiration study found fever, CRP, ESR, refusal to bear weight, and WBC helpful in high-suspicion children; CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) was a strong independent risk factor, and 3, 4, or 5 predictors gave 83%, 93%, and 98% probabilities.

criteria_options.label: Transient synovitis support: low-grade temperature <38.5 C, able to walk or weight bear with mild pain, serious causes excluded, rest/NSAIDs, and recovery expected over days to weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ describes transient synovitis as an exclusion diagnosis in a child with low-grade temperature <38.5 C who is usually able to walk or weight bear with mild pain; use rest/NSAIDs and safety-net when serious infection, SUFE, Perthes, fracture, and systemic disease are unlikely.

criteria_options.label: Age-specific hip disease: SUFE risk increases with weight >90th percentile and about 20% bilateral disease; Perthes concern is typical in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ highlights SUFE risk with weight >90th percentile, endocrine/puberty risk, and about 20% bilateral involvement requiring non-weight-bearing and prompt operative treatment; Perthes is considered especially in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp and may have normal early X-ray.
criteria_options.source_section: Slipped upper femoral epiphysis and Perthes disease

criteria_options.label: Bone/joint infection testing: FBE, ESR, CRP, and blood culture before antibiotics when feasible; early osteomyelitis X-ray can stay normal for 7-10 days; MRI with contrast is preferred when localized osteomyelitis remains possible
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH bone/joint infection guidance recommends FBE, ESR, CRP, blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, joint aspiration when appropriate without delaying antibiotics, X-ray recognizing early osteomyelitis may be normal for 7-10 days, ultrasound for effusion but not specificity, and MRI with contrast as preferred localized osteomyelitis imaging.

criteria_options.label: RCH bone/joint infection treatment: cefazolin 50 mg/kg max 2 g IV q8h; switch to cefalexin 45 mg/kg max 1.5 g q8h after 2-3 days IV when improving, afebrile, and markers improve; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH lists cefazolin 50 mg/kg (maximum 2 g) IV every 8 hours for uncomplicated bone/joint infection, oral cefalexin 45 mg/kg (maximum 1.5 g) every 8 hours, oral switch after 2-3 days IV if clinical improvement, afebrile, and improving inflammatory markers, and total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis or 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.

criteria_options.label: CHQ bone/joint infection oral switch/stopping: IV minimum 48 hours; afebrile at least 24 hours; CRP <20 mg/L or down >2/3 from peak; recheck CRP 1 week after oral switch and before stopping; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ recommends initial IV therapy for a minimum of 48 hours, oral switch when uncomplicated disease is clinically improved, afebrile at least 24 hours, tolerating oral intake, and CRP <20 mg/L or down by more than two-thirds from peak; recheck CRP one week after oral switch and before stopping, with total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis and 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.
data_needed: CRP one week after oral switch
criteria_options.source_section: IV-to-oral switch, monitoring, and treatment duration

criteria_options.label: Complicated bone/joint infection review: ID/AMS code within 24-48 hours for MRSA/complex disease; seek senior ID before IV beyond 2-3 days, PICC, neonate, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ requires ID review and antimicrobial stewardship authorization within 24-48 hours for MRSA or complex therapy contexts and senior ID input before longer-term IV/PICC plans beyond 2-3 days or for complex disease, neonates, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia.
criteria_options.source_section: Complex therapy, AMS authorization, and ID review

criteria_options.label: Occult toddler fracture support: negative initial radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture; follow-up radiographs found toddler fracture in 16 of 39 (41%) clinically presumed cases
criteria_options.criteria_text: ACR notes negative radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture and cites a series where 16 of 39 clinically presumed toddler fractures with negative initial imaging were seen on follow-up radiographs.
criteria_options.source_section: Radiography and occult toddler fracture discussion

criteria_options.label: CHQ limp safety-net: return for fever, worsening/nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, persistence >14 days, or tired/lethargic/unwell state
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ parent advice recommends re-presentation for fever, worsening or nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, symptoms persisting more than 14 days, or if the child is tired, lethargic, or unwell.

criteria_options.label: X-ray localized pain or injury area
criteria_options.criteria_text: X-ray localized pain or injury area Order radiography of the localized area of concern when pain, trauma, deformity, focal tenderness, or persistent inability to bear weight localizes a fracture or bone lesion concern. Positive or concerning imaging changes immobilization, orthopaedic referral, activity restriction, admission, or specialty workup. Fracture, dislocation, bone lesion, slipped hip pathology, or tumor causing localized pediatric limp. ACR rates radiography of the localized lower-extremity area as usually appropriate in a child up to age 5 with localized acute limp and no infection concern; RCH uses X-ray to exclude fracture or tumor.
criteria_options.source_section: variant 2 localized symptoms without infection and RCH X-ray comments

pediatric_msk_limp_hot_joint_systemic_mi.description: Use when the limp is not explained by isolated injury, transient synovitis, or focal infection and systemic, inflammatory, hematologic, neurologic, abdominal, or GU disease remains possible.

criteria_options.label: Low-risk limp: no investigations only if no red flags, mild/no discomfort with analgesia, and review plan within 7 days of onset
criteria_options.criteria_text: No investigations are indicated only when history/exam red flags are absent, the child ambulates with mild or no discomfort after simple analgesia, and a review plan exists within 7 days of limp onset if symptoms persist.

pediatric_msk_limp_hot_joint_monitoring_.description: Monitor pediatric limp or hot-joint management by reassessing the objective findings that selected the active branch.

criteria_options.label: Low-risk limp: no investigations only if no red flags, mild/no discomfort with analgesia, and review plan within 7 days of onset
criteria_options.criteria_text: No investigations are indicated only when history/exam red flags are absent, the child ambulates with mild or no discomfort after simple analgesia, and a review plan exists within 7 days of limp onset if symptoms persist.
data_needed: ability to weight bear after analgesia

criteria_options.label: Consult for limp lasting >7 days, inability to weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, systemic illness, malignancy features, or septic arthritis concern
criteria_options.criteria_text: Consult paediatrics/orthopaedics when symptoms last more than 7 days, the child cannot weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, is systemically unwell, has malignancy features, or septic arthritis is suspected.

criteria_options.label: Hip septic arthritis support: CRP >=20 mg/L and ultrasound effusion >=7 mm increase specificity/sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH cites evidence that CRP >=20 mg/L and hip ultrasound effusion >=7 mm has high specificity and sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis; use with clinical suspicion and orthopaedic consultation.

criteria_options.label: Kocher septic hip predictors: fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR >=40 mm/hr, WBC >12.0 x10^9/L; 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 predictors correspond to <0.2%, 3.0%, 40.0%, 93.1%, 99.6% predicted septic arthritis
criteria_options.criteria_text: Use Kocher predictors only as hip septic-arthritis risk support, not as discharge permission: history/temperature fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR at least 40 mm/hr, and WBC more than 12.0 x10^9/L; predicted septic arthritis rises from <0.2% with zero predictors to 99.6% with all four predictors.
criteria_options.source_section: Independent multivariate predictors and probability algorithm

criteria_options.label: Caird septic hip support: oral temperature >38.5 C and CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) increase concern; 3, 4, and 5 predictors correspond to 83%, 93%, and 98% septic arthritis probability in aspirated high-suspicion children
criteria_options.criteria_text: Caird prospective hip aspiration study found fever, CRP, ESR, refusal to bear weight, and WBC helpful in high-suspicion children; CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) was a strong independent risk factor, and 3, 4, or 5 predictors gave 83%, 93%, and 98% probabilities.

criteria_options.label: Transient synovitis support: low-grade temperature <38.5 C, able to walk or weight bear with mild pain, serious causes excluded, rest/NSAIDs, and recovery expected over days to weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ describes transient synovitis as an exclusion diagnosis in a child with low-grade temperature <38.5 C who is usually able to walk or weight bear with mild pain; use rest/NSAIDs and safety-net when serious infection, SUFE, Perthes, fracture, and systemic disease are unlikely.

criteria_options.label: Age-specific hip disease: SUFE risk increases with weight >90th percentile and about 20% bilateral disease; Perthes concern is typical in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ highlights SUFE risk with weight >90th percentile, endocrine/puberty risk, and about 20% bilateral involvement requiring non-weight-bearing and prompt operative treatment; Perthes is considered especially in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp and may have normal early X-ray.
criteria_options.source_section: Slipped upper femoral epiphysis and Perthes disease

criteria_options.label: Bone/joint infection testing: FBE, ESR, CRP, and blood culture before antibiotics when feasible; early osteomyelitis X-ray can stay normal for 7-10 days; MRI with contrast is preferred when localized osteomyelitis remains possible
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH bone/joint infection guidance recommends FBE, ESR, CRP, blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, joint aspiration when appropriate without delaying antibiotics, X-ray recognizing early osteomyelitis may be normal for 7-10 days, ultrasound for effusion but not specificity, and MRI with contrast as preferred localized osteomyelitis imaging.

criteria_options.label: RCH bone/joint infection treatment: cefazolin 50 mg/kg max 2 g IV q8h; switch to cefalexin 45 mg/kg max 1.5 g q8h after 2-3 days IV when improving, afebrile, and markers improve; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH lists cefazolin 50 mg/kg (maximum 2 g) IV every 8 hours for uncomplicated bone/joint infection, oral cefalexin 45 mg/kg (maximum 1.5 g) every 8 hours, oral switch after 2-3 days IV if clinical improvement, afebrile, and improving inflammatory markers, and total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis or 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.

criteria_options.label: CHQ bone/joint infection oral switch/stopping: IV minimum 48 hours; afebrile at least 24 hours; CRP <20 mg/L or down >2/3 from peak; recheck CRP 1 week after oral switch and before stopping; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ recommends initial IV therapy for a minimum of 48 hours, oral switch when uncomplicated disease is clinically improved, afebrile at least 24 hours, tolerating oral intake, and CRP <20 mg/L or down by more than two-thirds from peak; recheck CRP one week after oral switch and before stopping, with total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis and 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.

criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ requires ID review and antimicrobial stewardship authorization within 24-48 hours for MRSA or complex therapy contexts and senior ID input before longer-term IV/PICC plans beyond 2-3 days or for complex disease, neonates, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia.

criteria_options.label: CHQ limp safety-net: return for fever, worsening/nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, persistence >14 days, or tired/lethargic/unwell state
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ parent advice recommends re-presentation for fever, worsening or nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, symptoms persisting more than 14 days, or if the child is tired, lethargic, or unwell.

criteria_options.label: X-ray localized pain or injury area
criteria_options.criteria_text: X-ray localized pain or injury area Order radiography of the localized area of concern when pain, trauma, deformity, focal tenderness, or persistent inability to bear weight localizes a fracture or bone lesion concern. Positive or concerning imaging changes immobilization, orthopaedic referral, activity restriction, admission, or specialty workup. Fracture, dislocation, bone lesion, slipped hip pathology, or tumor causing localized pediatric limp. ACR rates radiography of the localized lower-extremity area as usually appropriate in a child up to age 5 with localized acute limp and no infection concern; RCH uses X-ray to exclude fracture or tumor.
criteria_options.source_section: variant 2 localized symptoms without infection and RCH X-ray comments

root.label: Pediatric limp/hot joint: route by instability, septic arthritis predictors, bone infection, injury, age-specific hip disease, systemic mimics, low-risk criteria, and safety-net needs

criteria.description: Activate for child or adolescent with limp, refusal to walk, non-weight-bearing, hot/swollen joint, suspected septic arthritis or osteomyelitis, limb pain, localized injury, hip/groin/thigh/knee pain, persistent limp, or clinician-chosen pediatric MSK evaluation.

root.action: Route pediatric limp and hot-joint presentations through missing-data, instability, septic arthritis, bone infection, fracture/safeguarding, SUFE/Perthes, systemic mimic, low-risk/no-test care, special-population review, monitoring, de-escalation, follow-up, and safety-net endpoints.

children.label: Internal data-availability guard

criteria.description: Route here when pediatric limp or hot-joint management cannot be classified because core history, exam, vitals, objective results, comorbidity, or follow-up data are unavailable.

children.action: Internal traversal guard for data completeness; hidden from the clinical pathway display.

display.reason: Internal traversal guard; exact missing fields remain in missing_data_needed.

missing_data_needed: trauma mechanism, timing, and safeguarding consistency
ability to walk or weight bear after analgesia
passive range of motion of affected and adjacent joints
immunocompromise, sickle cell, hemophilia, postoperative, or neonatal status
medication, allergy, recent antibiotic, and analgesia history
FBE/WBC, ESR, CRP, blood culture, aspiration, X-ray, ultrasound, or MRI results when indicated

children.label: Pediatric limp bedside assessment: age, duration, trauma/safeguarding, post-analgesia weight-bearing, fever/vitals, localization, ROM, labs/imaging, and follow-up
children.edgeLabel: Pediatric limp assessment bundle: No routine tests for low-risk comfortable limp; X-ray localized pain or injury area; Infection labs and blood culture route; Ultrasound hip effusion route; MRI for osteomyelitis localization; Unable to walk or weight bear; Fever with hot swollen limited joint; Systemically unwell or sepsis physiology; Night pain weight loss pallor bruising; Safeguarding concern or inconsistent injury; Measure temperature; Measure heart rate; Measure respiratory rate; Immediate orthopedic route for septic arthritis; Bone infection admission and antibiotic route; Low-risk discharge only when walking comfortably; Persistent limp or negative X-ray route; Referred or systemic cause route; Escalate acute inability to walk or weight bear until serious infection, fracture, neurologic disease, systemic disease, or unsafe discharge is unlikely.; Treat suspected septic arthritis as an orthopaedic emergency requiring urgent review, aspiration or washout planning, cultures, and IV antibiotics.; Admit suspected bone or joint infection for cultures, imaging, IV antibiotics, and orthopaedic or infectious diseases management.

criteria.description: Initial pediatric limp assessment requires age/development, duration, injury and safeguarding history, weight-bearing after analgesia, fever/vitals, focused MSK exam, infection/fracture/hip/systemic screen, targeted testing, and follow-up constraints.

criteria_options.label: Low-risk limp: no investigations only if no red flags, mild/no discomfort with analgesia, and review plan within 7 days of onset
criteria_options.criteria_text: No investigations are indicated only when history/exam red flags are absent, the child ambulates with mild or no discomfort after simple analgesia, and a review plan exists within 7 days of limp onset if symptoms persist.
data_needed: ability to weight bear after analgesia

criteria_options.label: Consult for limp lasting >7 days, inability to weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, systemic illness, malignancy features, or septic arthritis concern
criteria_options.criteria_text: Consult paediatrics/orthopaedics when symptoms last more than 7 days, the child cannot weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, is systemically unwell, has malignancy features, or septic arthritis is suspected.

criteria_options.label: Hip septic arthritis support: CRP >=20 mg/L and ultrasound effusion >=7 mm increase specificity/sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH cites evidence that CRP >=20 mg/L and hip ultrasound effusion >=7 mm has high specificity and sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis; use with clinical suspicion and orthopaedic consultation.

criteria_options.label: Kocher septic hip predictors: fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR >=40 mm/hr, WBC >12.0 x10^9/L; 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 predictors correspond to <0.2%, 3.0%, 40.0%, 93.1%, 99.6% predicted septic arthritis
criteria_options.criteria_text: Use Kocher predictors only as hip septic-arthritis risk support, not as discharge permission: history/temperature fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR at least 40 mm/hr, and WBC more than 12.0 x10^9/L; predicted septic arthritis rises from <0.2% with zero predictors to 99.6% with all four predictors.
criteria_options.source_section: Independent multivariate predictors and probability algorithm

criteria_options.label: Caird septic hip support: oral temperature >38.5 C and CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) increase concern; 3, 4, and 5 predictors correspond to 83%, 93%, and 98% septic arthritis probability in aspirated high-suspicion children
criteria_options.criteria_text: Caird prospective hip aspiration study found fever, CRP, ESR, refusal to bear weight, and WBC helpful in high-suspicion children; CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) was a strong independent risk factor, and 3, 4, or 5 predictors gave 83%, 93%, and 98% probabilities.

criteria_options.label: Transient synovitis support: low-grade temperature <38.5 C, able to walk or weight bear with mild pain, serious causes excluded, rest/NSAIDs, and recovery expected over days to weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ describes transient synovitis as an exclusion diagnosis in a child with low-grade temperature <38.5 C who is usually able to walk or weight bear with mild pain; use rest/NSAIDs and safety-net when serious infection, SUFE, Perthes, fracture, and systemic disease are unlikely.

criteria_options.label: Age-specific hip disease: SUFE risk increases with weight >90th percentile and about 20% bilateral disease; Perthes concern is typical in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ highlights SUFE risk with weight >90th percentile, endocrine/puberty risk, and about 20% bilateral involvement requiring non-weight-bearing and prompt operative treatment; Perthes is considered especially in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp and may have normal early X-ray.
criteria_options.source_section: Slipped upper femoral epiphysis and Perthes disease

criteria_options.label: Bone/joint infection testing: FBE, ESR, CRP, and blood culture before antibiotics when feasible; early osteomyelitis X-ray can stay normal for 7-10 days; MRI with contrast is preferred when localized osteomyelitis remains possible
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH bone/joint infection guidance recommends FBE, ESR, CRP, blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, joint aspiration when appropriate without delaying antibiotics, X-ray recognizing early osteomyelitis may be normal for 7-10 days, ultrasound for effusion but not specificity, and MRI with contrast as preferred localized osteomyelitis imaging.

criteria_options.label: RCH bone/joint infection treatment: cefazolin 50 mg/kg max 2 g IV q8h; switch to cefalexin 45 mg/kg max 1.5 g q8h after 2-3 days IV when improving, afebrile, and markers improve; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH lists cefazolin 50 mg/kg (maximum 2 g) IV every 8 hours for uncomplicated bone/joint infection, oral cefalexin 45 mg/kg (maximum 1.5 g) every 8 hours, oral switch after 2-3 days IV if clinical improvement, afebrile, and improving inflammatory markers, and total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis or 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.

criteria_options.label: CHQ bone/joint infection oral switch/stopping: IV minimum 48 hours; afebrile at least 24 hours; CRP <20 mg/L or down >2/3 from peak; recheck CRP 1 week after oral switch and before stopping; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ recommends initial IV therapy for a minimum of 48 hours, oral switch when uncomplicated disease is clinically improved, afebrile at least 24 hours, tolerating oral intake, and CRP <20 mg/L or down by more than two-thirds from peak; recheck CRP one week after oral switch and before stopping, with total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis and 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.
data_needed: CRP one week after oral switch
criteria_options.source_section: IV-to-oral switch, monitoring, and treatment duration

criteria_options.label: Complicated bone/joint infection review: ID/AMS code within 24-48 hours for MRSA/complex disease; seek senior ID before IV beyond 2-3 days, PICC, neonate, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ requires ID review and antimicrobial stewardship authorization within 24-48 hours for MRSA or complex therapy contexts and senior ID input before longer-term IV/PICC plans beyond 2-3 days or for complex disease, neonates, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia.
criteria_options.source_section: Complex therapy, AMS authorization, and ID review

criteria_options.label: Occult toddler fracture support: negative initial radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture; follow-up radiographs found toddler fracture in 16 of 39 (41%) clinically presumed cases
criteria_options.criteria_text: ACR notes negative radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture and cites a series where 16 of 39 clinically presumed toddler fractures with negative initial imaging were seen on follow-up radiographs.
criteria_options.source_section: Radiography and occult toddler fracture discussion

children.action: Activate pediatric sepsis, resuscitation, ED/ICU/orthopaedic transfer, and limb-threat pathways immediately. Obtain cultures if this does not delay antibiotics, start empiric antimicrobial treatment according to local pediatric sepsis and bone/joint infection guidance, provide analgesia, keep the child nil by mouth if operative drainage is possible, and defer low-risk discharge logic until physiology and limb perfusion are stable.

data_needed: ability to weight bear after analgesia

guideline_cutoffs.label: Consult for limp lasting >7 days, inability to weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, systemic illness, malignancy features, or septic arthritis concern
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Consult paediatrics/orthopaedics when symptoms last more than 7 days, the child cannot weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, is systemically unwell, has malignancy features, or septic arthritis is suspected.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Hip septic arthritis support: CRP >=20 mg/L and ultrasound effusion >=7 mm increase specificity/sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: RCH cites evidence that CRP >=20 mg/L and hip ultrasound effusion >=7 mm has high specificity and sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis; use with clinical suspicion and orthopaedic consultation.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Kocher septic hip predictors: fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR >=40 mm/hr, WBC >12.0 x10^9/L; 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 predictors correspond to <0.2%, 3.0%, 40.0%, 93.1%, 99.6% predicted septic arthritis
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Use Kocher predictors only as hip septic-arthritis risk support, not as discharge permission: history/temperature fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR at least 40 mm/hr, and WBC more than 12.0 x10^9/L; predicted septic arthritis rises from <0.2% with zero predictors to 99.6% with all four predictors.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Independent multivariate predictors and probability algorithm

guideline_cutoffs.label: Caird septic hip support: oral temperature >38.5 C and CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) increase concern; 3, 4, and 5 predictors correspond to 83%, 93%, and 98% septic arthritis probability in aspirated high-suspicion children
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Caird prospective hip aspiration study found fever, CRP, ESR, refusal to bear weight, and WBC helpful in high-suspicion children; CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) was a strong independent risk factor, and 3, 4, or 5 predictors gave 83%, 93%, and 98% probabilities.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Transient synovitis support: low-grade temperature <38.5 C, able to walk or weight bear with mild pain, serious causes excluded, rest/NSAIDs, and recovery expected over days to weeks
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ describes transient synovitis as an exclusion diagnosis in a child with low-grade temperature <38.5 C who is usually able to walk or weight bear with mild pain; use rest/NSAIDs and safety-net when serious infection, SUFE, Perthes, fracture, and systemic disease are unlikely.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Age-specific hip disease: SUFE risk increases with weight >90th percentile and about 20% bilateral disease; Perthes concern is typical in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ highlights SUFE risk with weight >90th percentile, endocrine/puberty risk, and about 20% bilateral involvement requiring non-weight-bearing and prompt operative treatment; Perthes is considered especially in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp and may have normal early X-ray.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Slipped upper femoral epiphysis and Perthes disease

guideline_cutoffs.label: Bone/joint infection testing: FBE, ESR, CRP, and blood culture before antibiotics when feasible; early osteomyelitis X-ray can stay normal for 7-10 days; MRI with contrast is preferred when localized osteomyelitis remains possible
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: RCH bone/joint infection guidance recommends FBE, ESR, CRP, blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, joint aspiration when appropriate without delaying antibiotics, X-ray recognizing early osteomyelitis may be normal for 7-10 days, ultrasound for effusion but not specificity, and MRI with contrast as preferred localized osteomyelitis imaging.

guideline_cutoffs.label: RCH bone/joint infection treatment: cefazolin 50 mg/kg max 2 g IV q8h; switch to cefalexin 45 mg/kg max 1.5 g q8h after 2-3 days IV when improving, afebrile, and markers improve; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: RCH lists cefazolin 50 mg/kg (maximum 2 g) IV every 8 hours for uncomplicated bone/joint infection, oral cefalexin 45 mg/kg (maximum 1.5 g) every 8 hours, oral switch after 2-3 days IV if clinical improvement, afebrile, and improving inflammatory markers, and total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis or 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.

guideline_cutoffs.label: CHQ bone/joint infection oral switch/stopping: IV minimum 48 hours; afebrile at least 24 hours; CRP <20 mg/L or down >2/3 from peak; recheck CRP 1 week after oral switch and before stopping; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ recommends initial IV therapy for a minimum of 48 hours, oral switch when uncomplicated disease is clinically improved, afebrile at least 24 hours, tolerating oral intake, and CRP <20 mg/L or down by more than two-thirds from peak; recheck CRP one week after oral switch and before stopping, with total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis and 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.
data_needed: CRP one week after oral switch
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: IV-to-oral switch, monitoring, and treatment duration

guideline_cutoffs.label: Complicated bone/joint infection review: ID/AMS code within 24-48 hours for MRSA/complex disease; seek senior ID before IV beyond 2-3 days, PICC, neonate, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ requires ID review and antimicrobial stewardship authorization within 24-48 hours for MRSA or complex therapy contexts and senior ID input before longer-term IV/PICC plans beyond 2-3 days or for complex disease, neonates, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Complex therapy, AMS authorization, and ID review

guideline_cutoffs.label: Occult toddler fracture support: negative initial radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture; follow-up radiographs found toddler fracture in 16 of 39 (41%) clinically presumed cases
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: ACR notes negative radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture and cites a series where 16 of 39 clinically presumed toddler fractures with negative initial imaging were seen on follow-up radiographs.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Radiography and occult toddler fracture discussion

guideline_cutoffs.label: CHQ limp safety-net: return for fever, worsening/nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, persistence >14 days, or tired/lethargic/unwell state
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ parent advice recommends re-presentation for fever, worsening or nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, symptoms persisting more than 14 days, or if the child is tired, lethargic, or unwell.

guideline_cutoffs.label: X-ray localized pain or injury area
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: X-ray localized pain or injury area Order radiography of the localized area of concern when pain, trauma, deformity, focal tenderness, or persistent inability to bear weight localizes a fracture or bone lesion concern. Positive or concerning imaging changes immobilization, orthopaedic referral, activity restriction, admission, or specialty workup. Fracture, dislocation, bone lesion, slipped hip pathology, or tumor causing localized pediatric limp. ACR rates radiography of the localized lower-extremity area as usually appropriate in a child up to age 5 with localized acute limp and no infection concern; RCH uses X-ray to exclude fracture or tumor.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: variant 2 localized symptoms without infection and RCH X-ray comments

children.action: Treat as an orthopaedic emergency. Discuss with orthopaedics and pediatrics immediately, obtain FBE/WBC, ESR, CRP, and blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, arrange joint aspiration with Gram stain/culture where appropriate, keep the child nil by mouth for possible washout, start empiric IV antibiotics when urgent treatment is needed rather than waiting for surgical timing, and admit or transfer to a center able to provide pediatric orthopaedic care.

criteria_options.criteria_text: ACR notes negative radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture and cites a series where 16 of 39 clinically presumed toddler fractures with negative initial imaging were seen on follow-up radiographs.
criteria_options.source_section: Radiography and occult toddler fracture discussion

criteria_options.label: CHQ limp safety-net: return for fever, worsening/nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, persistence >14 days, or tired/lethargic/unwell state
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ parent advice recommends re-presentation for fever, worsening or nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, symptoms persisting more than 14 days, or if the child is tired, lethargic, or unwell.

criteria_options.label: X-ray localized pain or injury area
criteria_options.criteria_text: X-ray localized pain or injury area Order radiography of the localized area of concern when pain, trauma, deformity, focal tenderness, or persistent inability to bear weight localizes a fracture or bone lesion concern. Positive or concerning imaging changes immobilization, orthopaedic referral, activity restriction, admission, or specialty workup. Fracture, dislocation, bone lesion, slipped hip pathology, or tumor causing localized pediatric limp. ACR rates radiography of the localized lower-extremity area as usually appropriate in a child up to age 5 with localized acute limp and no infection concern; RCH uses X-ray to exclude fracture or tumor.
criteria_options.source_section: variant 2 localized symptoms without infection and RCH X-ray comments

children.action: Admit or transfer for pediatric/orthopaedic management. Obtain FBE/WBC, ESR, CRP, blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, targeted X-ray to evaluate fracture/tumor while recognizing early osteomyelitis may be radiographically normal for 7-10 days, MRI with contrast when localized osteomyelitis or deep infection remains possible, drainage/surgery when abscess or septic focus requires it, empiric IV antibiotics using local antimicrobial stewardship, and ID review for complex disease.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Low-risk limp: no investigations only if no red flags, mild/no discomfort with analgesia, and review plan within 7 days of onset
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: No investigations are indicated only when history/exam red flags are absent, the child ambulates with mild or no discomfort after simple analgesia, and a review plan exists within 7 days of limp onset if symptoms persist.
data_needed: ability to weight bear after analgesia

guideline_cutoffs.label: Consult for limp lasting >7 days, inability to weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, systemic illness, malignancy features, or septic arthritis concern
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Consult paediatrics/orthopaedics when symptoms last more than 7 days, the child cannot weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, is systemically unwell, has malignancy features, or septic arthritis is suspected.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Hip septic arthritis support: CRP >=20 mg/L and ultrasound effusion >=7 mm increase specificity/sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: RCH cites evidence that CRP >=20 mg/L and hip ultrasound effusion >=7 mm has high specificity and sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis; use with clinical suspicion and orthopaedic consultation.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Kocher septic hip predictors: fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR >=40 mm/hr, WBC >12.0 x10^9/L; 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 predictors correspond to <0.2%, 3.0%, 40.0%, 93.1%, 99.6% predicted septic arthritis
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Use Kocher predictors only as hip septic-arthritis risk support, not as discharge permission: history/temperature fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR at least 40 mm/hr, and WBC more than 12.0 x10^9/L; predicted septic arthritis rises from <0.2% with zero predictors to 99.6% with all four predictors.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Independent multivariate predictors and probability algorithm

guideline_cutoffs.label: Caird septic hip support: oral temperature >38.5 C and CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) increase concern; 3, 4, and 5 predictors correspond to 83%, 93%, and 98% septic arthritis probability in aspirated high-suspicion children
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Caird prospective hip aspiration study found fever, CRP, ESR, refusal to bear weight, and WBC helpful in high-suspicion children; CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) was a strong independent risk factor, and 3, 4, or 5 predictors gave 83%, 93%, and 98% probabilities.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Transient synovitis support: low-grade temperature <38.5 C, able to walk or weight bear with mild pain, serious causes excluded, rest/NSAIDs, and recovery expected over days to weeks
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ describes transient synovitis as an exclusion diagnosis in a child with low-grade temperature <38.5 C who is usually able to walk or weight bear with mild pain; use rest/NSAIDs and safety-net when serious infection, SUFE, Perthes, fracture, and systemic disease are unlikely.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Age-specific hip disease: SUFE risk increases with weight >90th percentile and about 20% bilateral disease; Perthes concern is typical in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ highlights SUFE risk with weight >90th percentile, endocrine/puberty risk, and about 20% bilateral involvement requiring non-weight-bearing and prompt operative treatment; Perthes is considered especially in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp and may have normal early X-ray.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Slipped upper femoral epiphysis and Perthes disease

guideline_cutoffs.label: Bone/joint infection testing: FBE, ESR, CRP, and blood culture before antibiotics when feasible; early osteomyelitis X-ray can stay normal for 7-10 days; MRI with contrast is preferred when localized osteomyelitis remains possible
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: RCH bone/joint infection guidance recommends FBE, ESR, CRP, blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, joint aspiration when appropriate without delaying antibiotics, X-ray recognizing early osteomyelitis may be normal for 7-10 days, ultrasound for effusion but not specificity, and MRI with contrast as preferred localized osteomyelitis imaging.

guideline_cutoffs.label: RCH bone/joint infection treatment: cefazolin 50 mg/kg max 2 g IV q8h; switch to cefalexin 45 mg/kg max 1.5 g q8h after 2-3 days IV when improving, afebrile, and markers improve; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: RCH lists cefazolin 50 mg/kg (maximum 2 g) IV every 8 hours for uncomplicated bone/joint infection, oral cefalexin 45 mg/kg (maximum 1.5 g) every 8 hours, oral switch after 2-3 days IV if clinical improvement, afebrile, and improving inflammatory markers, and total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis or 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.

guideline_cutoffs.label: CHQ bone/joint infection oral switch/stopping: IV minimum 48 hours; afebrile at least 24 hours; CRP <20 mg/L or down >2/3 from peak; recheck CRP 1 week after oral switch and before stopping; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ recommends initial IV therapy for a minimum of 48 hours, oral switch when uncomplicated disease is clinically improved, afebrile at least 24 hours, tolerating oral intake, and CRP <20 mg/L or down by more than two-thirds from peak; recheck CRP one week after oral switch and before stopping, with total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis and 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.
data_needed: CRP one week after oral switch
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: IV-to-oral switch, monitoring, and treatment duration

guideline_cutoffs.label: Complicated bone/joint infection review: ID/AMS code within 24-48 hours for MRSA/complex disease; seek senior ID before IV beyond 2-3 days, PICC, neonate, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ requires ID review and antimicrobial stewardship authorization within 24-48 hours for MRSA or complex therapy contexts and senior ID input before longer-term IV/PICC plans beyond 2-3 days or for complex disease, neonates, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Complex therapy, AMS authorization, and ID review

children.action: Order site-specific radiographs, immobilize and provide analgesia when fracture is suspected, remember a negative initial X-ray does not exclude nondisplaced toddler fracture, arrange follow-up imaging or MRI when the child remains non-weight-bearing or pain localizes, restrict weight bearing as clinically needed, consult orthopaedics for growth-plate/dislocation/high-risk injury, and activate local safeguarding policy for inconsistent, delayed, recurrent, unwitnessed, or developmentally implausible injury.

criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH bone/joint infection guidance recommends FBE, ESR, CRP, blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, joint aspiration when appropriate without delaying antibiotics, X-ray recognizing early osteomyelitis may be normal for 7-10 days, ultrasound for effusion but not specificity, and MRI with contrast as preferred localized osteomyelitis imaging.

criteria_options.label: RCH bone/joint infection treatment: cefazolin 50 mg/kg max 2 g IV q8h; switch to cefalexin 45 mg/kg max 1.5 g q8h after 2-3 days IV when improving, afebrile, and markers improve; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH lists cefazolin 50 mg/kg (maximum 2 g) IV every 8 hours for uncomplicated bone/joint infection, oral cefalexin 45 mg/kg (maximum 1.5 g) every 8 hours, oral switch after 2-3 days IV if clinical improvement, afebrile, and improving inflammatory markers, and total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis or 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.

criteria_options.label: CHQ bone/joint infection oral switch/stopping: IV minimum 48 hours; afebrile at least 24 hours; CRP <20 mg/L or down >2/3 from peak; recheck CRP 1 week after oral switch and before stopping; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ recommends initial IV therapy for a minimum of 48 hours, oral switch when uncomplicated disease is clinically improved, afebrile at least 24 hours, tolerating oral intake, and CRP <20 mg/L or down by more than two-thirds from peak; recheck CRP one week after oral switch and before stopping, with total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis and 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.
data_needed: CRP one week after oral switch
criteria_options.source_section: IV-to-oral switch, monitoring, and treatment duration

criteria_options.label: Complicated bone/joint infection review: ID/AMS code within 24-48 hours for MRSA/complex disease; seek senior ID before IV beyond 2-3 days, PICC, neonate, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ requires ID review and antimicrobial stewardship authorization within 24-48 hours for MRSA or complex therapy contexts and senior ID input before longer-term IV/PICC plans beyond 2-3 days or for complex disease, neonates, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia.
criteria_options.source_section: Complex therapy, AMS authorization, and ID review

criteria_options.label: Occult toddler fracture support: negative initial radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture; follow-up radiographs found toddler fracture in 16 of 39 (41%) clinically presumed cases
criteria_options.criteria_text: ACR notes negative radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture and cites a series where 16 of 39 clinically presumed toddler fractures with negative initial imaging were seen on follow-up radiographs.
criteria_options.source_section: Radiography and occult toddler fracture discussion

criteria_options.label: CHQ limp safety-net: return for fever, worsening/nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, persistence >14 days, or tired/lethargic/unwell state
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ parent advice recommends re-presentation for fever, worsening or nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, symptoms persisting more than 14 days, or if the child is tired, lethargic, or unwell.

criteria_options.label: X-ray localized pain or injury area
criteria_options.criteria_text: X-ray localized pain or injury area Order radiography of the localized area of concern when pain, trauma, deformity, focal tenderness, or persistent inability to bear weight localizes a fracture or bone lesion concern. Positive or concerning imaging changes immobilization, orthopaedic referral, activity restriction, admission, or specialty workup. Fracture, dislocation, bone lesion, slipped hip pathology, or tumor causing localized pediatric limp. ACR rates radiography of the localized lower-extremity area as usually appropriate in a child up to age 5 with localized acute limp and no infection concern; RCH uses X-ray to exclude fracture or tumor.
criteria_options.source_section: variant 2 localized symptoms without infection and RCH X-ray comments

children.action: Examine the hip when knee or thigh pain is reported, obtain appropriate pelvic/hip imaging including frog-leg view when safe and indicated, make suspected SUFE non-weight-bearing and obtain prompt orthopaedic review for operative management, arrange orthopaedic referral for suspected Perthes or persistent limp in the typical age group even if early X-ray is normal, and avoid labeling adolescent hip or referred knee pain as a routine sprain without hip assessment.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Low-risk limp: no investigations only if no red flags, mild/no discomfort with analgesia, and review plan within 7 days of onset
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: No investigations are indicated only when history/exam red flags are absent, the child ambulates with mild or no discomfort after simple analgesia, and a review plan exists within 7 days of limp onset if symptoms persist.
data_needed: ability to weight bear after analgesia

guideline_cutoffs.label: Consult for limp lasting >7 days, inability to weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, systemic illness, malignancy features, or septic arthritis concern
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Consult paediatrics/orthopaedics when symptoms last more than 7 days, the child cannot weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, is systemically unwell, has malignancy features, or septic arthritis is suspected.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Hip septic arthritis support: CRP >=20 mg/L and ultrasound effusion >=7 mm increase specificity/sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: RCH cites evidence that CRP >=20 mg/L and hip ultrasound effusion >=7 mm has high specificity and sensitivity for pediatric hip septic arthritis; use with clinical suspicion and orthopaedic consultation.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Kocher septic hip predictors: fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR >=40 mm/hr, WBC >12.0 x10^9/L; 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 predictors correspond to <0.2%, 3.0%, 40.0%, 93.1%, 99.6% predicted septic arthritis
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Use Kocher predictors only as hip septic-arthritis risk support, not as discharge permission: history/temperature fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR at least 40 mm/hr, and WBC more than 12.0 x10^9/L; predicted septic arthritis rises from <0.2% with zero predictors to 99.6% with all four predictors.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Independent multivariate predictors and probability algorithm

guideline_cutoffs.label: Caird septic hip support: oral temperature >38.5 C and CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) increase concern; 3, 4, and 5 predictors correspond to 83%, 93%, and 98% septic arthritis probability in aspirated high-suspicion children
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Caird prospective hip aspiration study found fever, CRP, ESR, refusal to bear weight, and WBC helpful in high-suspicion children; CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) was a strong independent risk factor, and 3, 4, or 5 predictors gave 83%, 93%, and 98% probabilities.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Transient synovitis support: low-grade temperature <38.5 C, able to walk or weight bear with mild pain, serious causes excluded, rest/NSAIDs, and recovery expected over days to weeks
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ describes transient synovitis as an exclusion diagnosis in a child with low-grade temperature <38.5 C who is usually able to walk or weight bear with mild pain; use rest/NSAIDs and safety-net when serious infection, SUFE, Perthes, fracture, and systemic disease are unlikely.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Age-specific hip disease: SUFE risk increases with weight >90th percentile and about 20% bilateral disease; Perthes concern is typical in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: CHQ highlights SUFE risk with weight >90th percentile, endocrine/puberty risk, and about 20% bilateral involvement requiring non-weight-bearing and prompt operative treatment; Perthes is considered especially in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp and may have normal early X-ray.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Slipped upper femoral epiphysis and Perthes disease

children.action: Do not anchor on sprain or transient synovitis. Obtain FBE with film when malignancy or hematologic disease is possible, inflammatory markers and targeted rheumatology/infectious evaluation when morning stiffness/rash/recurrent fever is present, ECG and ARF workup when migratory arthritis or high CRP context suggests rheumatic fever, neurologic/abdominal/GU/scrotal exam and pathway referral when referred pain dominates, and admit or arrange urgent specialty review when systemic danger features are present.

criteria_options.label: Kocher septic hip predictors: fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR >=40 mm/hr, WBC >12.0 x10^9/L; 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 predictors correspond to <0.2%, 3.0%, 40.0%, 93.1%, 99.6% predicted septic arthritis
criteria_options.criteria_text: Use Kocher predictors only as hip septic-arthritis risk support, not as discharge permission: history/temperature fever >38.5 C, non-weight-bearing, ESR at least 40 mm/hr, and WBC more than 12.0 x10^9/L; predicted septic arthritis rises from <0.2% with zero predictors to 99.6% with all four predictors.
criteria_options.source_section: Independent multivariate predictors and probability algorithm

criteria_options.label: Caird septic hip support: oral temperature >38.5 C and CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) increase concern; 3, 4, and 5 predictors correspond to 83%, 93%, and 98% septic arthritis probability in aspirated high-suspicion children
criteria_options.criteria_text: Caird prospective hip aspiration study found fever, CRP, ESR, refusal to bear weight, and WBC helpful in high-suspicion children; CRP >2.0 mg/dL (>20 mg/L) was a strong independent risk factor, and 3, 4, or 5 predictors gave 83%, 93%, and 98% probabilities.

criteria_options.label: Transient synovitis support: low-grade temperature <38.5 C, able to walk or weight bear with mild pain, serious causes excluded, rest/NSAIDs, and recovery expected over days to weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ describes transient synovitis as an exclusion diagnosis in a child with low-grade temperature <38.5 C who is usually able to walk or weight bear with mild pain; use rest/NSAIDs and safety-net when serious infection, SUFE, Perthes, fracture, and systemic disease are unlikely.

criteria_options.label: Age-specific hip disease: SUFE risk increases with weight >90th percentile and about 20% bilateral disease; Perthes concern is typical in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ highlights SUFE risk with weight >90th percentile, endocrine/puberty risk, and about 20% bilateral involvement requiring non-weight-bearing and prompt operative treatment; Perthes is considered especially in males age 3-10 years with persistent limp and may have normal early X-ray.
criteria_options.source_section: Slipped upper femoral epiphysis and Perthes disease

criteria_options.label: Bone/joint infection testing: FBE, ESR, CRP, and blood culture before antibiotics when feasible; early osteomyelitis X-ray can stay normal for 7-10 days; MRI with contrast is preferred when localized osteomyelitis remains possible
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH bone/joint infection guidance recommends FBE, ESR, CRP, blood culture before antibiotics when feasible, joint aspiration when appropriate without delaying antibiotics, X-ray recognizing early osteomyelitis may be normal for 7-10 days, ultrasound for effusion but not specificity, and MRI with contrast as preferred localized osteomyelitis imaging.

criteria_options.label: RCH bone/joint infection treatment: cefazolin 50 mg/kg max 2 g IV q8h; switch to cefalexin 45 mg/kg max 1.5 g q8h after 2-3 days IV when improving, afebrile, and markers improve; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: RCH lists cefazolin 50 mg/kg (maximum 2 g) IV every 8 hours for uncomplicated bone/joint infection, oral cefalexin 45 mg/kg (maximum 1.5 g) every 8 hours, oral switch after 2-3 days IV if clinical improvement, afebrile, and improving inflammatory markers, and total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis or 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.

criteria_options.label: CHQ bone/joint infection oral switch/stopping: IV minimum 48 hours; afebrile at least 24 hours; CRP <20 mg/L or down >2/3 from peak; recheck CRP 1 week after oral switch and before stopping; total osteomyelitis 3-4 weeks and septic arthritis 2-3 weeks
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ recommends initial IV therapy for a minimum of 48 hours, oral switch when uncomplicated disease is clinically improved, afebrile at least 24 hours, tolerating oral intake, and CRP <20 mg/L or down by more than two-thirds from peak; recheck CRP one week after oral switch and before stopping, with total duration 3-4 weeks for osteomyelitis and 2-3 weeks for septic arthritis.
data_needed: CRP one week after oral switch
criteria_options.source_section: IV-to-oral switch, monitoring, and treatment duration

criteria_options.label: Complicated bone/joint infection review: ID/AMS code within 24-48 hours for MRSA/complex disease; seek senior ID before IV beyond 2-3 days, PICC, neonate, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ requires ID review and antimicrobial stewardship authorization within 24-48 hours for MRSA or complex therapy contexts and senior ID input before longer-term IV/PICC plans beyond 2-3 days or for complex disease, neonates, immunocompromise, Pseudomonas, relapse, or persistent bacteremia.
criteria_options.source_section: Complex therapy, AMS authorization, and ID review

criteria_options.label: Occult toddler fracture support: negative initial radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture; follow-up radiographs found toddler fracture in 16 of 39 (41%) clinically presumed cases
criteria_options.criteria_text: ACR notes negative radiographs do not exclude nondisplaced fracture and cites a series where 16 of 39 clinically presumed toddler fractures with negative initial imaging were seen on follow-up radiographs.
criteria_options.source_section: Radiography and occult toddler fracture discussion

criteria_options.label: CHQ limp safety-net: return for fever, worsening/nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, persistence >14 days, or tired/lethargic/unwell state
criteria_options.criteria_text: CHQ parent advice recommends re-presentation for fever, worsening or nocturnal pain, refusal to weight bear despite analgesia, no improvement after 3 days, symptoms persisting more than 14 days, or if the child is tired, lethargic, or unwell.

criteria_options.label: X-ray localized pain or injury area
criteria_options.criteria_text: X-ray localized pain or injury area Order radiography of the localized area of concern when pain, trauma, deformity, focal tenderness, or persistent inability to bear weight localizes a fracture or bone lesion concern. Positive or concerning imaging changes immobilization, orthopaedic referral, activity restriction, admission, or specialty workup. Fracture, dislocation, bone lesion, slipped hip pathology, or tumor causing localized pediatric limp. ACR rates radiography of the localized lower-extremity area as usually appropriate in a child up to age 5 with localized acute limp and no infection concern; RCH uses X-ray to exclude fracture or tumor.
criteria_options.source_section: variant 2 localized symptoms without infection and RCH X-ray comments

children.action: Avoid routine labs or imaging when the child meets low-risk criteria. Provide simple analgesia or NSAID/rest when transient synovitis is likely and contraindications are absent, document the working diagnosis and high-risk exclusions, discharge only if walking comfortably with or without analgesia and family can return, and arrange review within 7 days of onset if symptoms persist.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Low-risk limp: no investigations only if no red flags, mild/no discomfort with analgesia, and review plan within 7 days of onset
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: No investigations are indicated only when history/exam red flags are absent, the child ambulates with mild or no discomfort after simple analgesia, and a review plan exists within 7 days of limp onset if symptoms persist.
data_needed: ability to weight bear after analgesia

guideline_cutoffs.label: Consult for limp lasting >7 days, inability to weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, systemic illness, malignancy features, or septic arthritis concern
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Consult paediatrics/orthopaedics when symptoms last more than 7 days, the child cannot weight bear or permit movement after analgesia, is systemically unwell, has malignancy features, or septic arthritis is suspected.

children.action: Get pediatric, orthopaedic, infectious diseases, hematology, obstetric, safeguarding, or transfer-center review as appropriate. Apply local antimicrobial stewardship for MRSA/Pseudomonas/puncture or contaminated wounds, avoid routine oral-switch or discharge criteria in neonates, immunocompromised children, relapse, persistent bacteremia, hardware/postoperative cases, sickle cell/hemophilia pain, or uncertain safeguarding, and document the reviewer input needed before endpoint closure.
children.review_needed_reason: Local antimicrobial, orthopaedic, transfer, safeguarding, hematology, pregnancy/imaging, and postoperative protocols can override the general pediatric limp pathway.

children.label: Pediatric limp monitoring: pain, weight-bearing, fever, ROM, neurovascular status, cultures, CRP/ESR/WBC, imaging, antibiotic response, and discharge readiness

criteria.description: Monitor pediatric limp or hot-joint management by reassessing the objective findings that selected the active branch.
